----- DOC | Laya at Diwa | https://cvsu.edu.ph/laya-at-diwa/ | 2026-07-16 -----
LAYA AT DIWA
 CvSU Historical Landmark
A symbol of Truth, Excellence, Service

 Home > Laya at Diwa 
 ABOUT THE MONUMENT
 

 

 This brass artwork carries the University's ideals of TRUTH, EXCELLENCE and SERVICE.

“Laya at Diwa” is a creation of the Castrillos of Imus, Cavite (led and supervised by Jonnel P. Castrillo).

It was inaugurated on December 15, 2006 by Hon. Senator Edgardo J. Angara, on the occasion of the University's centenary celebration.

 

 There are five basic elements in the artwork together with the symbolic figures and meanings.

 Unchained Female
 

 The unchained female figure, holding a book, represents the empowered Filipino woman.

 She is beauty personified and is today's contemporary woman – a mother, a nurturer, an educator, and a humble servant to humanity.

 
 Male Figure
 

 The male figure with a pen and a torch represents the dignified Filipino father – strong, prominent, dignified and excellent in his field of endeavor besides being portrayed as persistent searcher of truth.

 The flame of the torch carries the letters CvSU for Cavite State University – serving as the guiding light of his noble quest.

 The interlocked arms of the man and the woman signifies unity of purpose and direction.

 
 Child
 

 The child figure, in a dynamic pose standing on top of the pillar and reaching out a dove, represents the youth in general and recognized as the proverbial hope of the future that needs molding and nurturing.

 The dove symbolizes peace and freedom.

 
 Central Pillar
 

 The central pillar symbolizes growth and development.

 It represents Filipinos' common aspiration for an improved quality of life – a kind of life with equity, peace, justice and harmony.

 The pillar is ever growing, directed higher but always deeply rooted in history as depicted by the murals on the sides of the base.

 The murals show the important historical events that took place in the province of Cavite and throughout the Philippines and portray events showing Filipino's life and spirit under imperial Spain and other colonizers including their continuing struggle for freedom and independence.

 
 Base
 

 The entire artwork is mounted on a CvSU logo-shaped base to serve as a fitting reminder to the entire CvSU community of its great responsibility to remain faithful to the University's vision and mission and to hold sacred its tenets of TRUTH, EXCELLENCE and SERVICE.

----- DOC | History | https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ | 2026-07-08 -----
Our History
 Rooted in a rich legacy of agriculture and education,Cavite State University has grown through generations of transformation, commitment, and service to the province and the nation.

 Home > History 
 A JOURNEY THROUGH TIME

 1906
 

 Indang Intermediate School

 1918
 

 Indang Farm School

 1927
 

 Indang Rural High School

 1958
 

 Don Severino National Agriculture School

 1964
 

 Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC)

 1998
 

 Cavite State University

 
 
 
 
 
 

 The Cavite State University (CvSU) has its humble beginnings in 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School with the American Thomasites as the first teachers. Several transformations in the name of the school took place; Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958. In 1964, the school was converted into a State College by virtue of Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC).

 On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No.8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University. In 2001, Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic, Cavite and Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario, Cavite, were integrated to the University by virtue of CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Since then, additional campuses in the province were established through memoranda of agreement with the LGUs. At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province. The main campus, approximately 70 hectares in land area and named as Don Severino delas Alas Campus, is in Indang, Cavite.

 CvSU is mandated “to provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction, and responsive research and development activities. It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness.”

 The University is offering close to 100 curricular programs in the undergraduate and graduate levels. It has more than 25,000 students and 1,200 faculty and staff from all campuses. The University is also authorized to certify and confer appropriate academic degrees in accordance with the Expanded Tertiary Education and Accreditation Program (ETEEAP). It has been accredited by TESDA as Trade and Testing Venue, and designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture. It also hosts the Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research and Development Consortium (STARDEC), the Affiliated Renewable Energy Center for Region IV-A, and the Regional ICT Center for Region IV-A.

 CvSU adheres to its commitment to Truth, Excellence and Service as it aims to be the “premier University in historic Cavite recognized for excellence in the development of globally competitive and morally upright individuals”.

----- DOC | Mandate, Mission, Vision, Core Values & Quality Policy | https://cvsu.edu.ph/mandate-mission-vision-core-values-quality-policy/ | 2026-07-08 -----
Mandate, Mission, Vision, Core Values, & Quality Policy
 Guiding our journey. Inspiring excellence.
Building a better future for Cavite and beyond.

 Home > Mandate, Mission, Vision, Core Values & Quality Policy 
 Vision Mission Core Values Quality Policy 
 Mandate

 Section 2 of Republic Act No. 8468 “An Act Converting the Don Severino Agricultural College in the Municipality of Indang, Province of Cavite into a State University, to be Known as the Cavite State University” states that,

 “ The University shall primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields, undertake research and extension services, and provide progressive leadership in these areas. ”

 Vision
 The premier university in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement.

 

 Mithiin ng Pamantasan
 Ang nangungunang pamantasan sa makasaysayang Kabite na kinikilala sa kahusayan sa paghubog ng mga indibidwal na may pandaigdigang kakayahan at kagandahang asal. 

 Mission
 Cavite State University shall provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction and responsive research and development activities. It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness.

 

 Hangarin ng Pamantasan
 Ang Cavite State university ay makapagbigay ng mahusay, pantay at makabuluhang edukasyon sa sining, agham at teknolohiya sa pamamagitan ng may kalidad na pagtuturo at tumutugon sa pangangailangang pananaliksik at mga gawaing pangkaunlaran. Makalikha ito ng mga indibidwal ng dalubhasa, may kasaysayan at kagandahan-asal sa pandaigdigang kakayahan. 

 Core Values

 Truth
 
 Integrity
 
 Excellence
 
 Service

 Quality Policy

 We C ommit to the highest standards of education, v alue our stakeholders, S trive for continual improvement of our products and services, and U phold the University's tenets of Truth, Excellence, and Service to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals.

----- DOC | CvSU achieves 95.56% passing rate in June 2026 Licensure Examination for Architects | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/06/29/cvsu-achieves-95-56-passing-rate-in-june-2026-licensure-examination-for-architects/ | 2026-06-29 -----
Cavite State University proudly celebrates the outstanding performance of its Main Campus in the June 2026 Licensure Examination for Architects (LEA), achieving a 95.56% passing rate, with 43 out of 45 examinees successfully earning their licenses—surpassing the national passing rate of 85.08%. 

 Congratulations to our newly licensed architects, the faculty members, and the College of Engineering and Information Technology for this remarkable achievement. Your dedication and pursuit of excellence continue to bring honor to the University. 

 See the full results here: https://www.prc.gov.ph/article/june-2026-licensure-examination-architects-results-released-two-2-working-days?fbclid=IwY2xjawSukAJleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETF6RTVuZWJlNFJBQ29pV1RRc3J0YwZhcHBfaWQQMjIyMDM5MTc4ODIwMDg5MgABHrR1A-2c-ZvDaBsCL9oLs8G_RgqRcQ6Jg3yRoMr65708aF6FPhCeoL7B8Z-Z_aem_NvDxBzSAmOR3iu1_8V15eQ

----- DOC | CvSU ranks 1001-1500th in THE Sustainability Impact Ratings | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/06/25/cvsu-ranks-1001-1500th-in-the-sustainability-impact-ratings/ | 2026-06-25 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) improved to 1001-1500 th ranking in the Times Higher Education (THE) Sustainability Impact Ratings 2026.  

 The THE Sustainability Impact Ratings assesses the educational institutions' research, stewardship, outreach, and teaching efforts that contribute to delivering the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).  

 From its previous 1501+ placement in the 2024 and 2025 THE Impact Rankings, the University also expanded its entries from four to 10 SDG categories in 2026. 

 Based on the figures released through the THE website, CvSU earned its highest scores in SDG 5: gender equality, 46.0-54.1 (401-600 th ); SDG 11: sustainable cities and communities, 48.5-55.4 (301-400 th ); and SDG 16: peace, justice and strong institutions, 54.3-59.1 (301-400 th ).  

 The University also improved its rankings in the following SDG categories:  

 
     SDG 1 (no poverty) 601-800 th 

     SDG 3 (good health and well-being) 1001+ 

     SDG 4 (quality education) 801-1000 th 

     SDG 8 (decent work and economic growth) 601-800 th 

     SDG 14 (life below water) 401+ 

     SDG 15 (life on land) 301-400 th 

     SDG 17 (partnerships for the goals) 1001-1500 th 

 
 CvSU Vice President for Research, Innovation, and Extension Dr. Melbourne R. Talactac, alongside Commission on Higher Education Commissioner Ricmar, received the Sustainability Award for the University during the Global Sustainable Congress at the Indonesia Convention Exhibition Indonesia Convention Exhibition (ICE), BSD City, Pagedangan, Tangerang Regency, Banten, Indonesia.

----- DOC | Congratulations to CvSU's 38 new CPAs | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/06/03/congratulations-to-cvsus-38-new-cpas/ | 2026-06-03 -----
Cavite State University recognizes the hard work, dedication, and perseverance of our examinees as we achieved a 57.58% overall performance rating in the May 2026 Certified Public Accountants Licensure Examination (CPALE). 

 According to the Professional Regulation Commission, a total of 3,004 out of 9,745 (30.85%) passed the CPALE given by the Board of Accountancy in the National Capital Region, Baguio, Butuan, Cagayan de Oro, Cebu, Davao, Iloilo, Koronadal, Legazpi, Lucena, Pagadian, Pampanga, Rosales, Tacloban, Tuguegarao, and Zamboanga in May 2026. 

 See full results here: https://prc.gov.ph/article/may-2026-certified-public-accountants-licensure-examination-results-released-four-4-working?fbclid=IwY2xjawSMS9pleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETFSWDZVMkFNdlRPdmNYaDhIc3J0YwZhcHBfaWQQMjIyMDM5MTc4ODIwMDg5MgABHiXSecpxW7h0bRZEtapYjtC2yQrR6-Xzk1Jg_zeFjb_EWwsRfHgIobF4lVpD_aem_D9RCZZUFWzbpZrYamjIDdw

----- DOC | Champions of Innovation: Honoring WURI 2026 Contributors | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/05/19/champions-of-innovation-honoring-wuri-2026-contributors/ | 2026-05-19 -----
In recognition of the collective efforts that strengthened Cavite State University's global standing in innovation, Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro, University President, led the awarding and recognition ceremony for the contributors to the World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI) 2026 on May 14, 2026. 

 The event recognized the writers, evaluators, coordinators, and various contributors whose dedication and collaboration played a vital role in the successful submission of the University's innovative programs and initiatives to the WURI 2026 rankings. 

 Through the collective commitment of the University community, CvSU successfully secured the 217th spot among the Top 500 Global Innovative Universities in the WURI 2026 rankings — a significant achievement that highlights the University's continuing pursuit of innovation, relevance, and transformative education on the international stage. 

 In her message, Dr. Nuestro expressed her gratitude to all contributors for their hard work, creativity, and commitment in showcasing the University's innovative practices and programs. She emphasized that the recognition reflects not only the excellence of individual offices and units, but also the collaborative spirit of the entire CvSU community. 

 

 The recognition ceremony reaffirmed CvSU's commitment to fostering innovation, advancing quality education, and strengthening its presence among globally recognized innovative universities.

----- DOC | CvSU congratulates its new Licensed Professional Teachers | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/05/14/cvsu-congratulates-its-new-licensed-professional-teachers-2/ | 2026-05-14 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) once again proved its excellence in teacher education after achieving remarkable results in the March 2026 Licensure Examination for Professional Teachers (LEPT) for both the Elementary and Secondary Levels. 

 Several CvSU campuses recorded passing rates significantly higher than the national average, earning recognition among the country's top-performing schools. 

 For the Elementary Level, CvSU Main Campus proudly secured a place among the Top 10 performing schools nationwide with an impressive 95.06% passing rate. 

 For the Secondary Level, CvSU Imus Campus ranked among the Top 3 performing schools nationwide after posting an outstanding 98.36% passing rate, while CvSU Silang Campus earned a spot among the Top 7 performing schools with a 96.97% passing rate. 

 Adding to this remarkable achievement, six CvSU graduates emerged as topnotchers in the March 2026 LEPT: 

 Elementary Level: 

 
 Erielle May Y. Rosell – CvSU Main Campus – Top 9 

 Maria Micaella S. Dabu – CvSU Cavite City Campus – Top 9 

 Sunday C. Bernal – CvSU Cavite City Campus – Top 10 

 
 Secondary Level: 

 
 Jane Paula C. Pagulong – CvSU Silang Campus – Top 9 

 Charisch E. Luardo – CvSU General Trias Campus – Top 9 

 Mark Lester V. Almerez – CvSU Carmona Campus – Top 10 

 
 Several campuses also achieved a perfect 100% passing rate, further reaffirming the University's unwavering commitment to academic excellence and quality teacher education. 

 See full results here: PRC Article

----- DOC | CvSU ranks 217th in WURI's top 500 Global Innovative Universities | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/05/08/cvsu-ranks-217th-in-wuris-top-500-global-innovative-universities/ | 2026-05-08 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) improved its global ranking to 217th place and placing within the Top 100 in 16 categories in the 2026 World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI). 

 WURI is a ranking system that assesses the societal and industry contributions of higher education institutions (HEIs), with focus on their innovative approaches to education, research, and society. 

 From ranking in two categories in its first WURI entry in 2024, the University is now among the Global Top 50 innovative universities in the following areas: 15th in Inclusive Social Innovation for the Underserved (C6); 18th in Social Impact through Knowledge Transfer (C5); 26th in Ethics and Integrity (A6) and University-Based Entrepreneurial Projects (C7); 38th in Industrial Application (A3); 42nd in Visionary Leadership (B1); 43rd in Interdisciplinary, Convergent, and Integrated Research (C2); and 46th in Crisis Management (A5). 

 CvSU also made it to the Global Top 100 in the following categories: 52nd in AI-Based Teaching and Learning Transformation (C4); 57th in Future-Oriented Responses to Global Uncertainty and Geopolitical Risk (A7); 62nd in Financial Impact-Driven Technology Transfer (C8); 74th in Funding for Sustainability (B5); 89th in Cost-Effectiveness Management (B7) and University Brand and Reputation (B8); 91st in SDG- and ESG-based Responses to Past and Present Global Challenges (A8); and 98th in Digital and AI Transformation in Strategy and Management (B3). 

 This remarkable achievement further strengthens the University's unwavering commitment to innovation and academic excellence by empowering learners, professionals, and stakeholders to create meaningful impact across industries and communities.

----- DOC | CvSU congratulates its new Registered Electrical Engineers, Master Electricians | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/04/30/cvsu-congratulates-its-new-registered-electrical-engineers-master-electricians/ | 2026-04-30 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) congratulates its new Registered Electrical Engineers and Master Electricians who successfully passed the April 2026 examinations.  

 The CvSU Main Campus registered an overall performance rating of 57.58% (19/33), while the CCAT Campus posted 75.93% (41/54) in the Registered Electrical Engineers Licensure Examination (REELE). 

 Jan Joseph Belen Lopez also made it to the top eight successful examinees in the REELE with a passing rate of 92.50%. 

 Morevover, the CvSU Main and CCAT campuses both recorded an overall performance rating of 60% in the Registered Master Electricians Licensure Examination (RMELE). 

 According to the Professional Regulation Commission, 3,769 out of 6,556 passed the REELE (57.49% national passing rate) while 493 out of 882 passed the RMELE (35.05% national passing rate) given by the Board of Electrical Engineering in the National Capital Region, Baguio, Butuan, Cagayan de Oro, Cebu, Davao, Iloilo, Koronadal, Legazpi, Lucena, Pagadian, Pampanga, Rosales, Tacloban, Tuguegarao, and Zamboanga.

----- DOC | CvSU awarded as one of DENR-EMB's top eco-friendly schools | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/04/23/cvsu-awarded-as-one-of-denr-embs-top-eco-friendly-schools/ | 2026-04-23 -----
Cavite State University's (CvSU) exemplary commitment to sustainability and environmental stewardship has been recognized during the 2025 National Search for Sustainable and Eco-friendly Schools (NSSEFS) of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources – Environmental Management Bureau (DENR – EMB). 

 This nationwide search highlights the sustainable and eco-friendly programs that are integrated into educational institutions' operations, curriculum, and student activities. It also aims to encourage the active and sustained participation of students, faculty members, and administrators in environmental issues, fostering an in-depth understanding and behavioral change within the academic community.  

 OIC Assistant Secretary for Environment and concurrent EMB Director Jacqueline A. Caancan, CESO III and EMB CALABARZON Regional Director Robert Angelo Metin led the national awarding ceremonies held on April 22, 2026 at Seda Vertis North Hotel in Quezon City.  

 CvSU officials received the prestigious Recognition of Excellence Award (College Category), Luntiang Puso Sustainability Award (Champion – College Category), and Water Leadership Award (College Category), together with PhP100,000 cash prize and electronic devices for the University.  

 The Water Leadership Award is given to educational institutions that have demonstrated exceptional commitment to water conservation and management as integrated in their school operations. The selection for this award is based on established water-saving programs, rainwater harvesting initiatives, wastewater treatment or recycling efforts, and other creative solutions for water management. Moreover, the initiatives are judged based on program plans and implementation including monitoring and evaluation, economic sustainability, presence of environmental partners and linkages, and gender equality and social inclusion.   

 Meanwhile, the Luntiang Puso Sustainability Champion Award is a special recognition that highlights the “heart” of the NSSEFS, specifically conferred upon the institution that achieved the highest overall rating among the top five finalists per category.  

 “The recognition is a testament to the exemplary leadership and coordination of school officials, led by Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro, President of Cavite State University, together with For. Junser P. Magpantay, NSSEFS Overall Coordinator, whose dedication and vision were instrumental in steering the university toward this distinguished achievement. CvSU's accomplishment highlights its strong commitment to promoting sustainability and environmental awareness within its academic community—principles that EMB Calabarzon consistently advances throughout the region,” EMB CALABARZON said in its social media post.

 ASSO. PROF. RONAN M. CAJIGAL

 MS. CATHERINE J. QUIÑONES

 Secretary of the CvSU Board of Regents 2nd Floor, Administration Building

 Cavite State University, Indang, Cavite

 Email: obs@cvsu.edu.ph

 
 𝗘𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗶𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗥𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀

 a. Be a Filipino citizen and of legal age;

 b. From an industry, business, or profession preferably related to the SUC's charter or mandate;

 c. Have at least five (5) years of employment, business, or practice of profession in the private sector;

 d. Be domiciled in or a resident for at least two (2) years of the city or province where the SUC or its campuses are located;

 e. Not connected with the SUC concerned or other government agencies and instrumentalities in any manner, for one (1) year immediately prior to his or her nomination to the PSR position;

 f. Not currently employed or appointed to any position in the government (local, national, including government-owned and controlled corporations), be it full time or part time, at the time of nomination for the PSR position;

 g. Not related to any member of the present Board or their duly-authorized representatives, within fourth civil degree of consanguinity or affinity; and,

 h. Not have been a candidate who lost in any national or local election, except for barangay elections, within one (1) year after such election.

 
 𝗗𝗼𝗰𝘂𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗥𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗶𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀

 a. Duly notarized application or nomination (with the conforme of the Nominee);

 b. Duly accomplished, computerized and notarized Personal Data Sheet (CSC Form 212);

 c. Certificate of Employment stating Nominee's present position in the private sector related to the industry, business or profession related to the SUC's charter or mandate;

 d. Service Record of Nominees who have been employed in the government at any point;

 e. NBI Clearance;

 f. Certification of No Connection with SUC the Nominee is applying or nominated to;

 g. Signed Commitment and Availability of the Nominee to serve as PSR;

 h. Signed Conforme to the Conditions of PSR as provided in Section 14; and,

 i. Other documents as may be required by the SUC GB.

 
 𝗦𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗮 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗖𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗲𝘀

 a. Prestige and prominence of the applicant or nominee

 b. Past and current membership in civic, social, religious, and professional organizations

 c. Past and current achievements in the field of specialization

 d. Knowledge, expertise, depth, and breadth of experience and business acumen

----- DOC | CvSU receives 5th IPOPHL Platinum, Palladium awards | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/03/25/cvsu-receives-5th-ipophl-platinum-palladium-awards/ | 2026-03-25 -----
The Cavite State University (CvSU), through its Innovations and Technology Support Office (ITSO), was honored with its fifth Platinum Award and first Palladium Award from the Intellectual Property Office of the Philippines (IPOPHL)   during the ITSO Presidents' Summit and General Assembly held on March 25, 2026, at the Diversion 21 Hotel in Iloilo City. 

 The University is the first higher education institution in the Southern Tagalog Region to receive the Palladium Award — the highest honor bestowed by the IPOPHL to top-performing ITSOs. This award recognizes those that have consistently demonstrated excellence, specifically by qualifying for the Platinum Award for five consecutive years or by securing a patent in a foreign country. 

 During the event, CvSU was also awarded a special citation for patents and trademarks. 

 The awarding was attended by CvSU President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro; Dr. Melbourne R. Talactac, Vice President for Research, Innovation and Extension; Dr. Agnes C. Francisco, Director of Knowledge Management Center (KMC); Dr. Teddy F. Tepora, Head of Intellectual Property and Technology Management Division and ITSO Manager; Mr. Gerald T. Viado, ITSO Technical staff; and Ms. Lecel Mojica, Executive Assistant of the University President. 

 With this year's theme “15 years of Strengthening the Philippine TISCs: Innovation and Creativity Toward Sustainable Technology Transfer,” the ITSO Presidents' Summit is a high-level gathering for Presidents and institutional heads intended to highlight leadership, strategic direction, and strengthened partnership with IPOPHL. 

 This activity also recognizes ITSOs with exemplary contributions to innovation, intellectual property (IP) filings, and technology transfer. 

 In 2015, CvSU's ITSO officially became part of the IPOPHL network. In 2017, the ITSO was institutionalized and established as a dedicated unit within the university's KMC. 

 The CvSU ITSO has evolved significantly since its establishment, becoming a key driver of IP and innovation at the University, culminating in its repeated receipt of the highest national awards. 

 Through these years, the University's ITSO has demonstrated a commitment to fostering innovation and protecting intellectual property, consistently receiving national recognition from the IPOPHL for its performance and output. The office works closely with the KMC to manage intellectual property and facilitate technology transfer. (with report from the Knowledge Management Center)

----- DOC | CvSU turns over seagrass research data to DENR, LGU Maragondon | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/03/24/cvsu-turns-over-seagrass-research-data-to-denr-lgu-maragondon/ | 2026-03-24 -----
The Cavite State University (CvSU) Research Center and the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment, and Natural Resources (CAFENR) officially turned over the seagrass data of Cavite to local government and environment officials, reflecting its commitment to promote data-driven policy making by providing scientific data through research to its stakeholders. 

 CvSU officials led the turnover of the results of the University's seagrass research studies to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) – Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Office (PENRO) Cavite and the Local Government Unit (LGU) of Maragondon during a ceremony themed “Rooted in Data, Safeguarding Cavite's Seagrass Ecosystem” held at the University Interdisciplinary Research Building, March 19, 2026. 

 This undertaking also highlighted the importance of partnerships among universities, national agencies, and local governments in addressing environmental issues and challenges. 

 In a special message, Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro, University President, underscored the University's pivotal role in the formulation of sustainable policies. 

 Mr. Glenn Bryan A. Creencia, CvSU's designated Faculty Researcher, then presented the outcomes of the seagrass research initiatives undertaken by the University. Seagrasses provide various ecosystem services, including food and habitat for many organisms. Additionally, they play a crucial role in oxygen production and carbon sequestration, making them essential in mitigating climate change. 

 Mr. Creencia rediscovered the last seagrass ecosystem of Cavite in February 2020. According to him, there are seven seagrass species present in the coastal waters of Maragondon. Moreover, the University also initiated conservation efforts through ex-situ seagrass propagation and transplantation. 

 In response, Ms. Judaline A. Fabro, Assistant Management Services Division Chief and in-charge of the Office of DENR-PENRO Cavite, and Mr. Noah M. Caasi, Municipal Administrator of LGU Maragondon, delivered their pledge of acceptance and commitment to the utilization of these research outputs in the development of their data-driven policies.  (with report from CAFENR and Research Center)

----- DOC | CvSU gets AACCUP Level III Institutional Accreditation status | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/03/11/cvsu-gets-aaccup-level-iii-institutional-accreditation-status/ | 2026-03-11 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) has been officially conferred with Institutional Accreditation (IA) Level III certification during the 2nd National Network of Quality Assurance Agencies (NNQAA), Inc. Quality Assurance Summit and Conferment Ceremony 2026, March 5-7, 2026, at the Heritage Hotel Manila in Pasay City.   

 The said award came after the University satisfactorily met the prescribed IA standards following a technical review and validation visit conducted by a team of evaluators from the Accrediting Agency of Chartered Colleges and Universities in the Philippines (AACCUP), Inc. on November 17-19, 2025.   

 Accepting the certification are University President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro and Quality Assurance Director Dr. Pia Rhoda P. Lucero, marking a significant milestone and a collective victory for the entire CvSU community with its first institutional accreditation.  

 Through this conferment, CvSU reaffirms its dedication to unwavering excellence and high standards across all institutional pillars: governance, academic instruction, research, community extension, and student services. 

 The comprehensive IA evaluation process assesses higher education institutions based on their effectiveness and compliance with established quality assurance requirements in nine key areas: (Area I) Governance and Management; (Area II) Teaching, Learning and Evaluation; (Area III) Faculty and Staff; (Area IV) Research; (Area V) Extension, Consultancy and Linkages; (Area VI) Support to Students; (Area VII) Library; (Area VIII) Infrastructure and other Learning Resources; and (Area IX) Quality Assurance Culture.

----- DOC | CvSU conducts public forum for ECC amendment | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/02/11/cvsu-conducts-public-forum-for-ecc-amendment/ | 2026-02-11 -----
As part of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process, Cavite State University (CvSU) conducted a public forum for the amendment of its Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) conditions, February 9, 2026, at the SM Rolle Hall.  

 During the public forum, CvSU, in coordination with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources – Environmental Management Bureau (DENR – EMB) CALABARZON, gathered community members, local government units, non-government organizations, and other stakeholders to discuss the proposed amendments and the status of the University's compliance with its ECC conditions. 

 The forum also provided a venue for CvSU and participating stakeholders to raise their concerns, identify issues, and recommend measures to address the impacts of the University's operations and ensure the protection and conservation of natural resources.  

 This undertaking emphasized the importance of public participation as a core component of the EIA process – promoting transparency, accountability, and informed decision making in ensuring the institution's compliance with environmental laws and regulations. 

 The DENR – EMB CALABARZON issued an ECC to CvSU on March 20, 2018 for the Don Severino de las Alas Campus in Brgy. Bancod, Indang, Cavite.  

 The EIA study for CvSU was led by Prof. Noel A. Sedigo as the Project Leader/Study Leader for Land, Dr. Lilibeth P. Novicio as the Study Leader for People, Dr. Amyel Dale L. Cero as the Study Leader for Water, and Ms. Ailene H. Calamlam as Study Leader for Air. The team is also supported by research assistants including Mr. Christian Dave M. Mendoza, Mr. Daeniel Zarene P. Mojica, Mr. John Daniel V. Cap-atan, Mr. Ayman S. Catenza, and Ms. Maria Trisha A. Cañete.

----- DOC | CvSU earns 3 QS Stars rating | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/02/10/cvsu-earns-3-qs-stars-rating/ | 2026-02-10 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) earned a three-star rating from Qacquarelli Symonds (QS) 2025, a globally recognized university rating system that evaluates institutions against international standards. 

 A three-star QS rating demonstrates an institution's strong overall performance, showing solid quality across teaching, facilities, employability, governance, and other key areas, with clear potential for further growth on the global stage. 

 Guided by CvSU ASCEND – Advancing Sustained Change and Excellence for National Development – the University earned the following ratings across key areas of global assessment:

 Employability – ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

 These results highlight CvSU's strength in instructional quality, governance, learning environments, and graduate outcomes, while reinforcing its commitment to inclusive growth, societal impact, and continuous improvement.

----- DOC | Employees, students, partners recognized during CvSU@120 celebration | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/02/10/employees-students-partners-recognized-during-cvsu120-celebration/ | 2026-02-10 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) celebrated academic excellence, exceptional public service, and strengthened collaborative partnerships for development during the Gawad Laya at Diwa and Partners' Recognition Day, January 28, at the International Convention Center. 

 As one of the highlights of the CvSU's 120th founding anniversary celebration, CvSU annually recognizes students, employees, retirees, organizational units, administrators, and partner agencies who demonstrated excellence, service, and meaningful achievements that help empower lives and communities.  

 “With the theme 'ASCEND with Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service: The Heart of CvSU's 120-Year Legacy,' we are reminded that our University's story is not measured by time alone, but by the values we uphold, the lives we transform, and the service we render to society,” said CvSU President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro in her message.  

 In his keynote speech, Bacoor City Mayor Strike B. Revilla emphasized that CvSU's 120 years of truth, integrity, excellence, and service is no small feat. 

 “Sa loob ng mahigit isang siglo, napatunayan ng CvSU na ang tunay na edukasyon ay hindi lamang tungkol sa paglikha ng mga propesyonal, kundi sa paghubog ng mga liders at mamamayan na may malasakit sa ating bayan,” Mayor Revilla said. 

 “Today, we celebrate not only the past, but the future. Ang Cavite State University ay patuloy na magiging ilaw ng kaalaman, haligi ng integridad, at tahanan ng mga bagong bayani ng bayan,” Mayor Revilla added. 

 University officials along with Mayor Revilla led in recognizing the employees and retirees who dedicated years of loyal service to CvSU, and demonstrated their perseverance and selfless commitment to go above and beyond the call of duty.  

 CvSU also honored its partner agencies whose support and collaboration has strengthened the institution's instruction, research, extension, and innovation initiatives. The awardees include Commission on Population and Development CALABARZON, Municipality of Alfonso, Municipality of Amadeo, National Grid Corporation of the Philippines, Philippine Coconut Authority, Don Mariano Marcos Memorial State University, University of the Philippines Manila, Pasay Harbor City Corporation, Verde Carbon Capture Inc., and Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources IV-A. 

 The top performing colleges and campuses recognized by the Professional Regulation Commission as well as CvSU's board topnotchers also took the spotlight – affirming the University's excellence in agriculture, social work, medical technology, criminology, veterinary medicine, and teacher education.  

 Meanwhile, Mr. Joseph Luke Emmanuel M. Ongliongco, a fourth year Bachelor of Science in Psychology student from the Bacoor Campus and currently the Deputy Student Regent of the Federation of Central Student Government, received the Most Outstanding Student of the Year award for consistently demonstrating exceptional academic and leadership skills, serving as role model to his fellow students.  

 Lastly, the CvSU Gawad Laya at Diwa Program PRAISE Awards honored distinguished faculty members, non-teaching employees, administrators, and organizational units that demonstrated strategic leadership, academic excellence, exemplary dedication to work, and significant contributions that create lasting impact to the University and the communities it serves:

----- DOC | CvSU marks 120th founding anniversary | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/02/02/cvsu-marks-120th-founding-anniversary/ | 2026-02-02 -----
The Cavite State University (CvSU) community gathered to commemorate the institution's 120 years of legacy of resilience – highlighting its mission to continuously empower more lives and communities from here and beyond. 

 Centered on the theme “ASCEND with Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service: The Heart of CvSU's 120-year Legacy,” University officials, faculty members, students, alumni, partners, and stakeholders come together in various activities slated on January 28-29, 2026. 

 The celebration kicked off with the ecumenical thanksgiving mass and the traditional wreath laying ceremony led by officials and the heirs of Don Severino de las Alas – an educator, public servant, and philanthropist who donated a portion of his land for the establishment of an intermediate school now known as CvSU. 

 During the opening program, CvSU President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro delivered her first State of the University Address where she highlighted the University's accomplishments and continuing efforts to achieve its CvSU ASCEND: 2025-2028 Strategic Framework focusing on transformational leadership, competent and committed workforce, administrative efficiency, and financial sustainability.  

 The 2025 goal for Institutional Transformation and Recognition is part of the four-year IDEAL Plan: Inclusive and Accessible Education; Dynamic and Competitive Research and Innovation; Empowered Communities and Stronger Partnership; Accountable and Client-Centered Governance; and Long-Lasting Sustainable Resource Generation.  

 In her message, Dr. Nuestro emphasized how the celebration affirms the University's enduring commitment to quality education and nation-building guided by its core values of Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service.  

 “For 120 years, CvSU has stood as a beacon of knowledge and service, empowering individuals and strengthening society. As we look back with gratitude and forward with determination, may this celebration remind us that CvSU's true legacy lies in the lives it has touched,” Dr. Nuestro said.  

 The 120th Founding Anniversary celebration also honored the notable contributions and unwavering commitment to academic excellence and public service of CvSU employees, students, retirees, and partner agencies during the annual Gawad Laya at Diwa and Partners' Recognition Day. 

 Meanwhile, as part of the efforts to promote employees' wellness and engagement, the academic and non-academic staff from the main and satellite campuses participated in the Employees' Star Olympics, zumba session, wellness day activities, and mini-concert.  

 Guests, students, and employees were also welcomed to the CvSU Agri-Eco Tourism Park, CvSU Historical and Cultural Museum, and other University sites during the Open House Visit. 

 Lastly, the members of the Administrative Council led the blessing of newly-constructed school buildings including the Boys' Dormitory; the Food Processing Building of the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources; and the STAR Building.

----- DOC | ESC orients student leaders on CvSU's volunteerism, service program | https://cvsu.edu.ph/blog/2026/01/27/esc-orients-student-leaders-on-cvsus-volunteerism-service-program/ | 2026-01-27 -----
Aimed at ensuring broad participation and collaboration across Cavite State University (CvSU), the Extension Services Center (ESC) conducted an orientation for student leaders on the Leaders Integrating Networks for General Aid Preparedness (LINGAP CvSU), January 8, at the Technology Demonstration Farm Training Hall. 

 Attended by representatives from various academic and non-academic student organizations at the Main Campus, the activity, held in two batches, provided the participants with an overview of LINGAP CvSU as the University's centralized hub for volunteerism and service initiatives. 

 In line with the institution's support to inclusive and sustainable development, the establishment of LINGAP CvSU has strengthened the University's efforts to respond more effectively to the needs of the communities. 

 According to the ESC, the community-led efforts, including outreach programs, relief-giving activities, educational workshops, clean-up drives, and health fairs, are now coordinated under the LINGAP CvSU program.  

 The orientation, led by Dr. Racquel G. Agustin, Director of ESC, and Engr. Arnelyn S. Romilla, Head of the Training Courseware and Development Division, highlighted the efforts to strengthen collaboration among students, reinforcing CvSU's commitment to social responsibility and civic engagement. ----- DOC | University Housing Facilities | https://cvsu.edu.ph/university-housing-facilities/ | 2025-11-05 -----
STATEMENT OF POLICY 

This policy shall govern the existing and newly-constructed housing facilities in the CvSU Employees' Village at the Don Severino delas Alas Campus of Cavite State University, Indang, Cavite.

This policy shall apply to all faculty and staff holding regular or permanent status of appointment and found qualified pursuant to the herein stipulated areas of concern.

The award of a housing unit is considered a privilege and is open and made available to CvSU employees that meet the qualifications and requirements to occupy, for a limited period. The said privilege can be revoked or withdrawn anytime, in accordance with terms and conditions of this policy and pertinent laws, rules, and regulations.

Employees granted the privilege are encouraged to acquire their own off-campus housing as their permanent residence upon termination of the housing privilege.

 QUALIFICATION CRITERIA FOR APPLICANTS 

CvSU employees who are qualified to apply for a housing facility must meet the following minimum criteria:

 
 holding permanent status of appointment at the time of application; and

 does not own a residential house within 10 km from the CvSU-Main campus.

 
 FILING OF APPLICATION 

Interested employee who wish to avail a housing facility shall fill up an Application Form (UHCE Form 1) to be submitted to the Office of the University President, which in turn shall forward the same to the Committee for evaluation and final recommendation.

The said application form may be downloaded from the University website or may be secured from the OVPBRG. The Committee, thru its Secretariat, shall provide the applicant with UHCE Form 2 or the Housing Applicant Information Sheet that will serve as the basis for evaluation, including other pertinent supporting documents like copy of the Service Record.

 Evaluation Process 

 
 Upon availability of a housing facility, the committee shall convene to evaluate the eligibility of the applicants, prepare a ranking and recommend the result of evaluation to the University President.

 All entries made in UHCE Forms 1 & 2 shall be considered as true information. Misrepresentation in any information shall be a ground for disqualification.

 The criteria for evaluation of applicants shall be the same for both academic and non-academic personnel, as follows:

 
 
 
 
 Criteria 
 Points 

 
  Service in the University, regardless of nature of appointment (years and months) 
 1 point per year 

 
 No. of dependents that lives with the applicant (spouse; unmarried children; and parents, if single) 
 1 point per dependent 

 
 Designations

 Dean, director or its equivalent

 Chairperson or unit head

 Other designations 
  

 8 points

 6 points

 4 points 

 
 Circumstances relevant to the interest of the University

 Health related positions

 Plants and animals' care; safety and security; power and water services; or food services 
  

 5 points

 4 points 

 
 Travel distance to Cavite State University

 More than 25 kilometers

 21 to 25 kilometers

 16 to 20 kilometers

 11 to 15 kilometers 
  

 5 points

 4 points

 3 points

 2 points 

 
 Notes:

 
 Only one designation shall be given score in the evaluation whichever is higher.

 Additional 5 points will be given if the qualified dependent is working in the University regardless of employment status.

 In case that both employee in a family applied for a housing unit, both could be evaluated separately but in no case shall their point be summed up.

 
 

 
 
 
 After evaluation the committee shall recommend to the University President through a resolution ALL the qualified applicants based on the ranks obtained from the points.

 
 NOTICE AND ACCEPTANCE OF AWARD 

 
 Upon approval of the University President, the committee shall issue a Notice of Award to the selected applicant.

 The applicant should sign in the Acceptance of Award to confirm their availment of the housing facility which should be returned to the Secretariat within three days.

 
 EXECUTION OF OCCUPANCY RIGHT AGREEMENT 

 
 Employees who are accepted the award for housing unit shall execute “Contract of Lease” with the University.

 The Contract shall be executed in at least six (6) copies, within ten (10) working days upon notice and acceptance of award. Such copies shall be distributed as follows: original copy for the Records Office, one copy each for the occupant, the Committee, the Business Affairs and Marketing Office (BAMO) and 2 copies for the Notary Public.

 The employee shall be allowed to move in upon submission of the three (3) copies of the notarized contract to the Secretariat.

 
 CLASSIFICATION OF HOUSING FACILITY 

There shall be three (3) types of housing facilities in the University classified as follows:

 Class A: Permanent housing facility constructed in 2023 onwards designed for a family.

Class B: Old housing facility designed for a family.

Class C: Shared housing facility for bachelors/bachelorettes.

 ALLOCATION OF HOUSING FACILITY 

 
 There shall be an executive housing facility to be automatically assigned to the University President while the Vice-Presidents may avail a housing facility upon request to the President, provided that there is an available unit. Their term of stay shall be co-terminus with their respective term of office. The President may avail and shall be given priority in other unit upon completion of the term of office. Former Vice Presidents may also apply to continue the housing privilege subject to evaluation based on the criteria as stated in this Policy.

 There shall be a reasonable distribution of housing units among the academic and non-academic personnel, at the rate of 60% and 40% respectively.

 The distribution ratio must be considered to determine the priority group for the award of available housing. However, in case of vacancy, the vacant facility shall be offered to another qualified applicant should there be no one qualified to fill in and meet the ratio either for academic or non-academic employee.

 
 TENURE OF OCCUPANCY 

 
 Employees who may have the privilege to stay in the housing facility shall be given a maximum of ten (10) years. After which, it is assumed that the employee had established an own residence. The Contract of Lease should be for a period of two (2) years, subject to renewal. They shall be permitted to enjoy such right until their contract expires or has been terminated, or their services to the University was terminated either by resignation or retirement.

 Occupants with expired contract upon approval and effectivity of this revised Policy shall be determined immediately and an “Order to Vacate” shall be served by the Committee within fifteen (15) days. The Accounting Unit will make sure that the due accounts of these occupants on housing occupancy shall be collected through salary deduction. Likewise, occupants who shall resist the Order to Vacate shall be charged a penalty twice the current rental rate of the housing facility they are occupying.

 For the occupants who are still qualified to stay but fails to renew the Contract for a period of three (3) months after its expiration, the contract shall be deemed terminated and an 'Order to Vacate' shall be issued. After which, it is assumed that the occupant has established an own abode and the facility shall be deemed vacant. In such case, Section 12.2 shall also be applied.

 Occupants whose nature of stay was found not in accordance with this rule, upon investigation shall be considered illegal occupant, hence, they shall be issued an “Order to Vacate” and shall be executed within fifteen (15) days from date of receipt. All unpaid rents computed based on their length of stay upon investigation shall be collected through salary deduction.

 A 'Notice of Termination' shall be served by the Committee to the occupants concerned not later than sixty (60) days prior to the termination of the contract to give such occupant an ample time to settle their household, or to renew their contract.

 Occupants who shall be separated from the service with the University through retirement, death, disability or any other causes, their household shall voluntarily and automatically vacate the unit within six (6) months from the date of effectivity of such cause of separation.

 Occupants who consumed the maximum of ten (10) years may apply for an extension of only one (1) year subject to the approval of the President.

 Any occupant who left the facility vacant for a period of six (6) months shall be deemed voluntarily terminated the Contract. As such, the unit shall be declared vacant.

 Any occupant who commits violations of any provision in the existing Policies and Guidelines, after proven through investigations shall: (1) be warned in the first offense; (2) not be qualified for the renewal of Contract in the second offense; and (3) render the Contract terminated and shall be issued an 'Order to Vacate' the facility immediately in the third offense.

 The occupant who was able to establish an own abode shall voluntarily surrender the facility to the Committee and likewise shall voluntarily terminate the Contract to give chance to other applicants to enjoy the privilege.

 
 RENTAL RATES AND MODE OF PAYMENT 

 
 The monthly rent for each housing unit shall be determined according to the existing rate.

 A monthly payment of rent shall be required through salary deduction during the first (1st) quincena payroll.

 Upon signing of the contract, the occupant shall be required to make a deposit equivalent to two (2) months rental to absorb unavoidable arrears/defaults in payment, as well as to serve as reserve for clean-up work once the unit is vacated. However, these amounts shall be refunded to the occupant upon termination of the contract, the former if he has no outstanding account due for the University and the latter if he does the cleaning of the unit and its surrounding, repaired the damages, and replaced the broken and unrepairable items, prior to departure. Otherwise, the same shall be forfeited accordingly. The amount so collected in these deposits shall be kept in an authorized depository bank as a trust fund earning interest. Such interests shall be used to absorb deflationary costs in the performance of repair and maintenance of the housing facility.

 The schedule of rental rates for housing facility are as follows:

 For water services, the following rate schedule shall apply; subject to increase of 5% per annum to cover inflation rate provided the rate shall not exceed the prevailing rate charged by the Local Water District of Indang.

 For electricity, the electrical services at the CvSU Employees Village are provided by MERALCO, hence, MERALCO rates shall apply. Occupants whose electric services are supplied by the University power system shall be charged according to MERALCO rates. The electric meter shall be provided by the University. For Class C, they will be charged a fixed rate of P300.00 per occupant.

 
 MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS 

 
 The occupant and immediate family (spouse and unmarried children, or parents, if single) are the only authorized occupants of the unit irrespective of head counts. However, smaller family unit could be allowed to accommodate other relatives or domestic helpers provided that when the number of occupants exceeds six (6), they shall be charged P200.00 per month for each additional resident. The monthly rent for each housing unit shall be determined according to the existing rate.

 The occupant shall be held liable for the conduct of their household members resulting to any damages.

 As the leased unit shall be used exclusively for private residence of the occupant and immediate family members, the occupant is automatically not authorized to sublease the unit.

 No alteration, addition or other improvements within the building shall be made by the occupant without the written approval of the University upon recommendation of the Committee and once approved, all improvements or addition shall become part and property of the University. Total costs incurred by the occupant shall be made deductible to the monthly rental subject to submission of receipts of all the materials used in the said improvement upon review of the Committees. However, improvement outside the building including garage and dirty kitchen may be requested and recommended by the Committee but shall be at the own expense of the occupant and will not be deducted from the monthly rental.

 The leased premises shall be kept clean and orderly at all times. Quarterly inspection and monitoring shall be conducted by the Business Affairs and Marketing Office for the purpose. An inspection report shall be submitted to the Committee for review and appropriate action.

 Unnecessary disturbances such as emanating from blaring radios, TV sets, videoke, car horns, firecrackers and any other disturbing noise shall be avoided particularly during rest periods.

 No permanent plants shall be removed by the occupant upon termination of the lease without prior approval of the Committee.

 Unless authorized by the Committee, no animals such as pets, poultry and livestock shall be kept on the leased premises.

 No transfer to another housing unit of CvSU Employees' Village shall be allowed except upon approval by the Committee after due verification of the unit and validation of the grounds of transfer.

 No highly inflammable or explosive materials or chemicals shall be kept in the premises. Provided, fire arms kept at the housing facility are properly licensed by the authorized agency.

 Occupants are bound to follow university rules that have bearing to the proper utilization of the housing units or to the occupancy which includes strict prohibition on possession and/or use of illegal drugs and illegal gambling, excessive use of plastic and Styrofoam food containers and display of pornographic materials.

 In no case, should an occupant engage in business in the housing unit nor put signages to that effect.

 Occupants are required to report early signs of termite incursion to the BAMO and/or PPS for appropriate action.

 Occupants are expected to abide with the solid waste management system of the University and refrain from open burning of their garbage.

 Occupants shall exercise professional standards of social and moral conducts to serve as models in the community.

 
 TRANSFERABILITY 

The right of the occupant to the housing facility is not transferable and the usual procedure in awarding a housing unit shall be followed should the spouse or any of the children of the occupant desires to occupy the unit upon their retirement.

 TRANSITORY PROVISION 

 
 Applicants with pending applications filed at the Committee before the effectivity of these policy and guidelines shall be required to re-confirm their applications under these rules.

 All occupants whose contract is in accordance with 2009 Revised Policy, their contract shall be renewed January of the following year upon approval of this policy and shall adapt the new rules and regulation hereof.

----- DOC | Grievance Committee Annual Report | https://cvsu.edu.ph/grievance-committee-annual-report/ | 2025-10-08 -----
Grievance Committee Report

(For the Year 2024) 

  

 Cavite State University (CvSU) upholds the principles of accountability, transparency, and ethical conduct in all aspects of its operations. In line with ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management System standards and Civil Service Commission (CSC) requirements, CvSU maintains both internal grievance mechanisms and external accountability measures to ensure that concerns are addressed fairly, promptly, and consistently. 

 This report summarizes the governance and ethics-related activities undertaken by the University in 2024. 

 The Grievance Committee provides employees and stakeholders with a formal venue to raise workplace issues and administrative concerns, ensuring resolution in accordance with Civil Service Commission (CSC) policies. 

 The Committee convenes as needed to ensure a timely response to grievances. In 2024, a total of seven (7) meetings were held to address submitted complaints, which included deliberations on cases, and mediation sessions. 

 Summary of Internal Cases Handled (2024) 

 
 
 
 TYPE OF CASE 
 NUMBER OF CASES 
 STATUS 

 
 Workplace grievance 
 1 
 Resolved 

 
 Total 
 1 
   

 
 
  

 Moreover, as part of its commitment to good governance and ethical accountability, Cavite State University also actively responds to complaints and concerns filed through the 8888 Citizen's Complaint Center, a government platform for reporting issues against public offices. 

 Upon receipt of a complaint, the Office of the Vice President for Administrative and Support Services forwards the matter to the concerned office or employee for response and corrective action. The concerned office then prepares a reply, which is carefully reviewed and submitted through the portal within the prescribed timeframe. 

 To ensure transparency and accountability, copies of responses and resolution reports are also filed and monitored. 

 Summary of 8888 Complaints (2024)

 For the year under review, Cavite State University achieved a 100% resolution rate for all internal grievances and 98.36% for 8888 complaints, ensuring that concerns were addressed promptly and effectively. This consistent responsiveness has contributed to improving stakeholder trust, as the timely resolution of issues reflects the University's commitment to accountability and good governance.

GRIEVANCE COMMITTEE CALENDAR

 The University has institutionalized the Grievance Committee as a key non-research ethics body in compliance with national regulations and internal governance policies. The Committee addresses general administrative complaints and employee concerns in accordance with Civil Service Commission (CSC) guidelines, ensuring fairness, accountability, and ethical conduct in resolving disputes. 

 The Committee convenes upon receipt of complaints and maintains a standing monthly schedule to meet as necessary. These meetings may address actual grievance or misconduct cases, or serve as venues policy review or preparing consolidated monitoring reports even during months when no cases are filed.

 The Committee to deliberate on the issues including the letter from Faculty A sent on November 21, 2024. 

  

 After thoroughly reviewing the accounts provided by all parties involved, the Committee has determined that the issue has already significantly impacted Faculty A's relationship with the individuals concerned, and has also given rise to additional matters requiring resolution. 

  

 Moreover, Faculty A had already taken her complaint against Faculty B to court, which further complicates the possibility of a resolution through the Grievance Committee.

----- DOC | Sustainable Procurement | https://cvsu.edu.ph/sustainable-procurement/ | 2025-10-08 -----
Cavite State University (CvSU) upholds the principles of transparency, accountability, efficiency, and sustainability in all its procurement activities, in accordance with New Government Procurement Act (NGPA) Republic Act No. 12009, amending the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184), and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. 

 CvSU integrates sustainability principles in its purchasing and contracting practices by: 

 
 promoting the efficient use of resources and environmentally responsible procurement; 

 ensuring compliance with environmental, social, and ethical standards set by law; and 

 encouraging suppliers and contractors who demonstrate sustainable and responsible practices.

----- DOC | Foreign Students | https://cvsu.edu.ph/foreign-students/ | 2025-10-08 -----
INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS OF UNITED NATIONS 

 ORGANIZATION VISION 

 The International Students of United Nations hoped to unite with the university as they model all foreign students in the university as to lift up higher the vision of the university in developing globally competitive and morally upright individuals.

 ORGANIZATION MISSION 

 International Students of United Nations shall comply with all academic rules and regulations of the university as to achieve the vision and mission set forth by university. It shall uphold and utilize all relevant and educational opportunities in colleges of the university as to promote and protect the high reputation built by the university since 1906 till date. It shall produce law abiding and peaceful candidates who will serve as crusaders to attract other foreigners into the university.

 AIM and OBJECTIVES 

 Sequel to, and in support of the vision, mission and by-laws, our aims and objectives is to have a unified body of foreign students who will support and encourage each other as to maintain the focus that led us into Philippines and much more into Cavite State University. And also to take our studies more serious in order to graduate with honors were possible as to make our parents, sponsors, supporters, our countries, instructors, professors and the university proud.

 HISTORY 

 The dream of setting up an organization on behalf foreign students in Cavite State University was hatched on a good day that Mahel ThankGod A. perused through student handbook and found out on page 5, 6,  were the handbook states that when foreign students are more than 5, an adviser should be designated to look after their welfare. Based on this academic rules and regulations, the said name and person above met with the dean of student's affairs with the sole aim to inquire if foreign students in the university had any adviser. However, at the end of meeting with the dean, records showed that there was none and on that juncture, the dean promised that an organization will put in place and also appoint someone to man it.

 Hence, we wish to thank the dean and cavite State University immensely for fulfilling their promise which gave birth to International Students of United Nations (ISUN).

 The said organization was formerly inaugurated on 15 th  August, 2012 and Mahel ThankGod Amukele a second year student of College of Arts and Sciences taking up Bachelor of Arts in English was recommended to be the President of the organization and the recommendation was ratified on 28 th  August, 2012 general meeting and election of officers of ISUN. The organization had its first general meeting on August 28, 2012 and the following officer were elected and dedicated into office.

----- DOC | College of Arts and Sciences – Programs Offered | https://cvsu.edu.ph/college-of-arts-and-sciences-programs-offered/ | 2025-09-02 -----
Bachelor of Arts in Communication 

 Program Description  

 BA Communication is a field of study that covers the various ways by which humans communicate. It focuses on how verbal and non-verbal messages are used to create meanings in different contexts using diverse media platforms. It includes a broad range of subject matter, ranging from the study of communication in interpersonal relationships, groups, organizations, and cultures. 

 Objectives  

 The program aims to: 

 
 equip students with the knowledge and skills in the theory and practice of communication applicable in various professions and contexts, with a focus on the media professions; 

 uphold professional and ethical standards in communication while being sensitive to cultural diversity and social responsibility through exposure to industry or corporate exposure; 

 conduct research to analyze communication issues, develop innovative solutions, and contribute to the body of knowledge in the field of communication, and  

 demonstrate mastery of communication theories, principles and practices applicable to various professions and contexts but are not limited to public and corporate communication/information officers, social science and communication researchers/analysts, communication/media planners and managers, program/project development officers, public relations and advertising practitioners, media practitioners, learning system designers, multimedia designers, and allied/related professions. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Science in Development Communication 

 Program Description  

 Bachelor of Science in Development Communication is a field of study that covers the various ways by which humans communicate. It focuses on how verbal and non-verbal messages are used to create meanings in various media platforms. It includes a broad range of subject matter from communication, natural and social sciences, and other technical courses such as agriculture, health, and environment. This also includes the study of communication in interpersonal relationships, groups, organizations, and cultures.   

 Objectives 

 The program aims to: 

 
 equip students with the knowledge and skills needed in teaching, managing, and implementing communication programs for development; 

 cultivate a critical understanding of development perspectives and the ability to analyze and address complex issues in communication for development; 

 guide student-communicators who uphold ethical standards and practices in their professional endeavors through industry and corporate exposure; and  

 develop graduates who may find employment in, but not limited to public and corporate communication/information officers, social science and communication researchers/analysts, communication/media planners and managers, program/project development officers, media practitioners, learning system designers, multimedia designers, and in the allied/related professions. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Science in Biology 

 Program Description 

 The Biological Science Program for BS Biology students is designed to equip graduates with skills and knowledge in human resources that will enhance the progress of this agriculturally based country, depending on their specialization.  In the said program, the course curriculum is systematically given from first to fourth year levels, assuring that BS Biology students will be given all necessary subjects to be used once they are in the field. There are lots of private and government-owned and controlled corporations and research institutions that can readily make use of their knowledge in their chosen career. An increasing number of industries are now becoming allied to biological sciences, especially to the pharmaceutical, food, and scientific supply manufacturing. 

 Objectives 

 The program is structured to meet the needs of professional biologists who: 

 
 can be employed in government/private institutions and other agencies where scientists with biological expertise are needed; 

 can engage in entrepreneurial activities; 

 conduct research in various areas in biology; 

 undertake postgraduate education in Biology and allied fields; and 

 pursue a career in teaching. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Arts in Journalism  

 Program Description 

 Bachelor of Arts in Journalism covers the principles and techniques of reporting, writing, producing, and disseminating news in a range of forms, formats, and platforms in a democracy such as the Philippines. It focuses on timely events, issues, and developments relevant to the communities the journalist serves. This field of study promotes professional and ethical standards and includes reflection on best practices. 

 Objectives 

 The students in this program: 

 
 are equipped with knowledge, skills, attitudes, values, ethics, and competence that professionals need; 

 have the capacity to anticipate and respond to the needs of rapidly developing media; and 

 may pursue the following career paths, but not limited to broadcast journalists, media researchers, news analysts, news organization representatives, public broadcasting agents, public relations practitioners, photojournalists, proofreaders, program directors, reporters, website developers, and writers. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Arts in English Language Studies 

 Program Description 

 Bachelor of Arts in English Language Studies offers courses that are designed to integrate theory and practice to prepare students for effective communication in English in diverse contexts and situations. 

 Objectives 

  The program aims to: 

 
 provide a comprehensive knowledge of the English language, its origin, growth and development, structures, and use; 

 enhance the student's competencies in the use of the English language in real-world contexts; and 

 present appropriate strategies of language use through a heightened awareness of how English works in different situations in the Philippines, in Asia, and the rest of the world.  

 
  

 Bachelor of Science in Psychology 

 Program Description 

 This program is designed to provide students with knowledge-based methods, content areas, and essential theories in the field of psychology. It seeks to facilitate the development of intellectual skills and characteristics that enhance the students' lifelong personal and career success. The program begins with a General Psychology course and includes courses in quantitative methods and experimental designs. Additional coursework is selected from a broad range of available courses.  Students are encouraged to pursue breadth across the areas of Psychology, particularly when planning for advanced study in Psychology. The department does, however, identify sets of courses particularly relevant for students interested in clinical, school, or industrial/ organizational psychology. 

 Objectives 

  The program aims to: 

 
 produce graduates who can apply psychological principles in analyzing structures of society; 

 systematically equip students with a foundation of theories and practice for real-life activities, research, and extension services; 

 yield critical thinkers and creative workers for professional global competitiveness in educational, industrial, and clinical settings; 

 cultivate professionally competent, morally upright, and socially responsible individuals exposed to various psychological situations; and 

 hone future psychometricians, psychologists, counselors, human resource managers, educators, and other psychological-helping professionals able to respond to the demands of a growing society. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Arts in Political Science 

 Program Description 

 Political Science, as a discipline, delves into multifarious and intricate activities of man as well as political, economic, social, and cultural processes. Students are trained for careers in politics, law, teaching, civil service, and foreign service. Furthermore, students are equipped with knowledge and skills in discharging their rights and obligations as members of a democratic community, to prepare them to be vigilant, dynamic, and civic-spirited citizens. 

 Objectives  

 The program aims to: 

 
 compare, contrast, and classify the major concepts in the discipline; 

 recognize and define the distinctiveness of the discipline, its subfields, theories, and methods; 

 design and execute research inquiries using appropriate quantitative and qualitative methods guided by the theories or conceptual framework; 

 demonstrate written, visual, and oral presentation skills to produce and present analytic reports; 

 critique, judge, and response to current issues and problems based on a substantive understanding of the historical and contemporary developments in the national and global context; and 

 develop a predisposition towards ethical political involvement and practice in various forms and on different levels of engagement. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Science in Social Work 

 Program Description 

 The Bachelor of Science in Social Work (BSSW) program of Cavite State University is in accordance with CMO No. 39 s. 2017.  The social work program prepares students for professional entry-level social work practice.  It is geared towards the preparation of students for generalist social work practice. Professional education in social work requires the acquisition and application of a beginning level of knowledge, attitudes, values, and skills in enhancing the social functioning of individuals, families, groups and communities, linking people (client systems) with needed resources, improving the operation of social service delivery networks, and promoting social justice through organizing and advocacy. 

 Objectives 

 Upon successful completion of the program, its graduates are expected to: 

 
 pass the board exam for social workers; 

 be equipped with knowledge, attitudes, values, and skills necessary for a beginning professional generalist practitioner in working with individuals, families, groups, organizations, and communities; 

 promote biological, psychological, social, and spiritual growth and development and people empowerment through various social activities; 

 develop partnerships nationally and internationally with government and non-government agencies, and/or enhance the college's existing resources and services; and 

 be globally competitive and morally upright individuals responsive to the needs and demands of society. 

 
  

 Bachelor of Science in Applied Mathematics 

 Program Description 

 The curriculum for the BS Applied Mathematics is built around a traditional base of foundational or core courses in the major areas of mathematics and applied mathematics with the inclusion of specialized courses in mathematics, applied mathematics, relevant disciplines, and emerging areas. 

 Objectives 

 The program aims to: 

 
 Provide the students with a curriculum that shall enhance their mathematical and critical thinking skills, and develop in them a greater appreciation and understanding of the importance of mathematics in history and in the modern world; 

 Prepare the students for advanced studies in mathematics, applied mathematics, and related fields; 

 Train students and develop them to become competent and well-equipped instructors to teach mathematics and statistics in college; 

 Prepare students for mathematics-oriented careers in industry, business, and public administration; 

 Guide students in conducting scientific researches relevant to the thrusts of the Department, College, and the University; and 

 Encourage students to render extension services to the stakeholders in line with the program. 

 
  

 This page was last edited on 02 September 2025.

 C ater relevant general education subjects and specialization courses in the arts and sciences to develop critically minded and morally upright individuals capable of meeting national and global demands; 

 A dvance sustainable research and extension in the arts and sciences responsive to the institutional, regional, and global thrusts; and
