----- DOC | CoN holds 1st int'l webinar on health info | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/09/30/con-holds-1st-intl-webinar-on-health-info/ | 2020-09-30 -----
It takes a collaborative effort to make one great leap. Through partnerships, College of Nursing was able to make it as it hosted its first international webinar last August 29, 2020.

 With the support from the University administration headed by CvSU President Dr. Hernando D. Robles, the Beta Nu Delta Nursing Society for Humanity, Filipino Nursing Diaspora Network and the CvSU College of Nursing led by the dean, Dr. Evelyn M. Del Mundo, the activity was able to share with the participants the changing developments and updates in providing quality health care education among all the nurses and other allied health care professionals around the world.

 “Correct information can save lives, misinformation and fake news kill,” Dr. Robles said in his inspirational message with emphasis on the relevance of the webinar with the COVID-19 pandemic the world is currently facing.

 Dr. John Robert Bautista, a Bullard Research Fellow at the School of Information, University of Texas at Austin served as the guest speaker for the said activity. He presented and shared one of his funded researches which helped him pursue his post-doctoral degree in Communication Science at the Wee Kim Wee School of Communication and Information, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. With his numerous scholarly achievements, both in local and international engagements, Dr. Bautista remained humble and very enthusiast in helping and mentoring health care professionals to produce research innovation initiatives that address problems in the allied health community. One particular example is the so-called infodemic or surge of either good or bad information.

 As the world comes to grip with the effects of this global health crisis, his research work highlighted that the battle is not only with the allied health care professionals which combat the virus but also with the social media users who are dealing with misinformation. He emphasized that incorrect and misleading facts and information being posted and shared in any social networking site worldwide can put vulnerable individuals in danger.

 Rolando P. Antonio, college research coordinator who served as one of the reactors in the said webinar, suggested the use of conceptualized models by Dr. Bautista in correcting health misinformation in social media to produce more scientific and evidence-based nursing practices in relation to patient care and to teach the nursing students be more online literate, accurate and responsible in general.

 Meanwhile, Ms. Karen Louela Rint and Mr. Jerome Babate, forum moderators, facilitated the Q & A portion.

 “The practical tips can be short term or long term. Short term is that, as we social media users and as health care professionals, we can actually start with our community in terms of our relative- the use of group chat that sometimes tend to post health misinformation. We may want to deal with them first before we can go public, to share more correct information so that instead of false information, the corrected ones will be seen by many. But for the long term tip, we need to emphasize on health literacy. Unfortunately, literacy in the Philippines is not that high,” answered Dr. Bautista when asked about his practical tips and proposed solutions for managing fake medical information.

 The challenge is now on the application and implementation of the research outcome that is recommended also to be embedded in the academe as a special topic in teaching health education for nursing and other allied health courses.

 Moreover, recognizing this notable undertaking is just a beginning of other collaborations in the future with the prestigious organization, headed by Prof. Violeta Lopez and the Cavite State University – College of Nursing since both value the trust of its constituents in the academe and in nursing profession. Future activities also aim to inspire medical frontliners in fulfilling their mission and vision imbued with the necessary knowledge and skills with confidence and integrity to provide quality health care services to the global community. ( Hazel Joyce L. Guiao ) . 

 Poster for the activity 
(Source: Filipino Nursing Diaspora Network FB page)

----- DOC | To all the students and parents | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/09/06/to-all-the-students-and-parents/ | 2020-09-06 -----
ADVISORY 

 

 
 To all the students and parents,

 

 
 Cavite State University, like any other academic institutions, government agencies and private entities in the country and the world, is experiencing a great challenge brought by the COVID-19 pandemic. It is a test to the ability and preparedness of not only the officials of the government and the University, but also of the students and their families. This has affected our daily activities, our jobs and even the process of teaching and learning.

 
 This coming Monday, September 7, 2020, the University and all its branches around the province will start the semester. Flexible learning modalities approved by the Board of Regents by virtue of Resolution No. 18 dated August 6, 2020 will be implemented.

 
 Flexible learning modalities is a combination of both online/offline, modular and other methods of delivering instruction to enhance students' knowledge. This is the first time it will be used by the University. We know that no one can be considered yet as an expert in this flexible learning in the midst of the pandemic, hence, we seek for your utmost patience and support.

 
 For parents, please do not solely depend on the efforts of the teachers and the University for the learning of the students. In the same way, teachers must also be more considerate of the situation of their students. Partnership is what we need.

 
 Rest assured that the University will do its best to continue giving quality education like before. Trainings and orientations for teachers in relation to innovative ways of facilitating learning, including the preparation of modules and the use of the free learning management system, are almost done.

 
 We have also improved the internet infrastructure with enhanced bandwidth and the use of other technologies that will help the students as they gain new knowledge and skills this semester. With the help of the Department of Information and Communications Technology, the University will be providing free “wi-fi connection” in all its branches. DICT has placed the needed infrastructure which is expected to be used this September.

 
 With all the challenges we are facing, the University is once again asking for cooperation from all sectors – the teachers, the staff, the students, the parents and the local government and all its relevant branches – as we continue pursuing quality education amidst the pandemic. Meanwhile, the University will strive to bring joy in the students' campus life even with the uncertainties brought by this situation.

 
 To all the students, do not lose hope, but instead, study hard. Focus on the lessons to be facilitated by the teachers and we will do our best to support you.

 
 Despite this situation, we want to warmly welcome you to the Academic Year 2020-2021.

 
 In God's merciful grace, “we will heal and learn as one”.

----- DOC | Payment of Fees thru Landbank OnColl | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/09/03/payment-of-fees-thru-landbank-oncoll/ | 2020-09-03 -----
ADVISORY 

 
 For students who need to pay certain fees, please follow the procedure for over-the-counter transactions through Landbank.

 
 1. Proceed to any Land Bank Branch near you (NCR & Region IV A & B).

 
 2. Fill out the OnColl Payment Slip using the following details:

 

 
 Account Name CAVSU OnColl

 Account Number 1502-2220-03

 Reference 1 Your Name

 Reference 2 Student Number

 Reference 3 Campus code and Nature of Payment

 01-Main Campus

 02-Silang Campus

 03-Carmona Campus

 04-Bacoor Campus

 05-Trece

 06-Tanza

 07-GenTri

 
 Service charge amounting to P25.00 per transaction is imposed by Land Bank.

 

 
 3. After payment, email the picture or scanned copy of the validated deposit slip to cashiersoffice@cvsu.edu.ph.

 
 4. After verification of payment, the Cashier's Office will send the scanned copy of the Official Receipt.

 
 Payment thru bank transfer or online transfer of any kind is not allowed.

 
 Thank you.

----- DOC | DISCLAIMER | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/08/20/disclaimer-2/ | 2020-08-20 -----
We would like to inform all incoming first year students that the so-called “University First Year Student Council” using Laya at Diwa image in its Facebook page is NOT AN OFFICIAL student organization recognized by CvSU.

 The University has the Central Student Government and student councils being recognized every academic year but their terms had already ended and the new officers are to be recognized after a legitimate process as approved by the Office of Student Affairs. Hence, we would like to make it clear that “University First Year – Student Council” activities are not officially approved by the University. Membership to such is voluntary but not recognized by CvSU.

 Please be guided accordingly.

 Thank you.

----- DOC | DISCLAIMER | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/08/20/disclaimer/ | 2020-08-20 -----
It has come to our attention that a certain “CvSU JAMbagan 2020”, a fund-raising activity for the benefit of CvSU students who are in need, is set to be conducted as an online concert this coming August 29.

 While donation drives are helpful in preparing for the classes this September, we would like to inform you that this activity is NOT OFFICIAL, hence the use of the names of CvSU, Central Student Government and CvSU Musikeros is not allowed. Every student organization is recognized every academic year, hence, the use of such during vacation and while students are still enrolling is considered NOT OFFICIAL. Any fundraising activity from “not yet recognized” organizations do not have “official permission” from the University.

 A letter dated August 17, 2020 was sent by the Office of Student Affairs to the former vice president of the Central Student Government clearly explaining that the term of office of CSG officers has ended last July 31, 2020 as emphasized in the CSG-CBL, Article XII, Section 4.

 With the good intention of the activity, the organizers are given the following options: 1) to do it this coming First Semester, AY 2020-2021 after the recognition of student organizations; or 2) to do it as an individual/group fund-raising but without the use of CvSU's name and the said organizations.

 We promote bayanihan in this time of crisis and we are in support of initiatives that extend assistance to the students but it has to be made clear to participating individuals if the activity is officially from CvSU or a privately-organized donation drive.

 Thank you.

----- DOC | To all the parents and students | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/08/14/advisory-to-all-the-parents-and-students/ | 2020-08-14 -----
ADVISORY 

 To all the parents and students,

 We would like inform you that all the campuses of Cavite State University will open the classes this coming September 7, 2020. Because of the pandemic, no face-to-face classes will be conducted and this type of teaching may only be done on the second semester (January 2021), depending on the decision of IATF, CHED and the LGU.

 With the flexible mode of teaching, a combination of online and offline classes (through modules) will be implemented. No class will be held as purely online. This is to give opportunities for other students who may not have the capacity to participate in the said mode. All of these measures will be explained further before classes start through the orientations.

 AGAIN, THE UNIVERSITY WILL NOT IMPLEMENT A PURELY ONLINE MODE OF TEACHING.

 Thank you and we hope for the safety of everyone.

 
 PAUNAWA 

 Sa mga Magulang at Mag-aaral:

 Nais po naming ipabatid sa inyong lahat na ang Cavite State University at mga sangay nito sa buong probinsya ay magbubukas ng klase sa ika-7 ng Setyembre 2020.

 Dahil sa pandemya, wala pong magaganap na face-to-face na klase sa buong semester. Maaari lamang magkaroon ng ganitong paraan ng pagtuturo sa darating na ikalawang semestre (Enero 2021) subalit ito ay nakabatay pa rin sa rekomendasyon ng ng IATF, CHED at LGU.

 Ang pamantasan ay gagamit ng flexible na pamamaraan sa pagtuturo. Ito po ay kumbinasyon ng ONLINE at OFFLINE o sa pamamagitan ng MODULES.

 Wala pong klase na gagawin na purong ONLINE. Ito po ay upang mabigyan ng pagkakataon ang mga mga mag-aaral na walang kapasidad sa nasabing pamamaraan.

 Ang lahat po ng panuntunang ito ay muli naming ipaliliwanag sa inyo bago magpasukan sa pamamagitan ng mga oryentasyon.

 MULI PO NAMING IPINABABATID NA ANG PAMANTASAN AY HINDI GAGAMIT NG PURONG ONLINE NA PAMAMARAAN.

 Maraming salamat po at manatili po sanang ligtas ang lahat.

Isang mapagpalang araw sa inyong lahat!

 Inihahandog ng GAD Resource Center simula ngayong buwan ng Agosto ang PARTNERS IN GAD (Partnership in AwaReness TraiNing for Engaged and Responsible Stakeholders in Gender And Development) na naglalayong makapaghatid ng kaalaman tungkol sa konsepto, mga isyu, at mga batas patungkol sa GAD (Sex and Gender, Gender-Fair Language, VAWC, Sexual Harassment, at iba pa). ----- DOC | Cavite Under MECQ | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/08/03/cavite-under-mecq/ | 2020-08-03 -----
ADVISORY

 Please be advised that Cavite has been placed again under the modified enhanced community quarantine (MECQ) to further prevent the spread of COVID-19. In this regard, the University shall revert to providing skeleton workforce starting this Tuesday, August 4 to 18, 2020 .

 Meanwhile, all Physical Plant Services personnel shall continue to complete the 7-day quarantine extension until Aug 10, 2020 before he/she can report for work if they belong to the skeleton workforce of their respective units.

 All other employees shall be on work-from-home arrangement and shall come to the University only if needed and upon approval of the Crisis Manager and/or the President. With this, all University transactions within the said period shall be purely online.

 Please be reminded that all employees are enjoined to observe proper health protocol both inside and outside the campus.

 For widest dissemination.

 Thank you.

----- DOC | Advisory for all employees, both skeleton and work-from-home | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/07/23/advisory-for-all-employees-both-skeleton-and-work-from-home/ | 2020-07-23 -----
All employees, both skeleton and work-from-home, are reminded to strictly observe health protocols at all times. Those with cold, cough or even slight fever shall not report for work. Employees who feel sick, particularly those who had close contact to COVID-19 patients outside the University, shall immediately report to Dr. Romeo Sanchez, University Physician, or the nearest Rural Health Unit for guidance.

 Self-monitoring and self-restraining are necessary in caring for ourselves and others as well.

 Thank you.

----- DOC | Announcement: Admission for SY 2020-2021 | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/07/07/announcement-admission-for-sy-2020-2021/ | 2020-07-07 -----
CAVITE STATE UNIVERITY- MAIN 

 Announcement: Admission for SY2020-2021 

 Out of 35,297 applicants who registered online, only 7,460 applicants can be admitted in the Main Campus due its limited carrying capacity.

 List of Qualified Applicants for College Admission 

 First Semester, SY 2020-2021 

 
 You can confirm your slot beginning July 8 – 19, 2020. Failure to confirm your slot within the given period will mean forfeiture of your application for the course. 

 
 
 For those qualified applicants who have already confirmed their slot, kindly wait for the registration process that will start on July 27, 2020.

 
 
 For those qualified applicants who are in the waiting list* – you are advised to confirm your slot beginning July 20-26, 2020.

 
 *applicants in the waiting list can confirm their intention to wait for vacant slots in their respective courses. Applicants in the waiting list do not have any confirmed slots but will only be given once a qualified applicant in their program fail to confirm their slot.

 Procedure for confirmation of your slot:  

 
 Using your registered email account, log-in to the Online Student Admission System – https://registrar.cvsu.edu.ph/osas/

 
 
 Go to the ADMISSION RESULT section. Update your personal information.

 
 
 Read the Notice of Admission (NOA) stating the course that you are qualified to enrol in

 
 
 Answer the survey form for internet connectivity and ICT readiness

 
 
 Click the “CONFIRM” button to confirm slot

 
 
 Qualified applicants of the following courses –  BS Hospitality Management, BS Tourism Management, BS Medical Technology, and BS Nursing are subject for College Interview. Please confirm your  slot for the interview  by undergoing the same procedure as posted above and wait for the announcement of the respective Colleges on how they will inform/contact you for interview.

 
 
 
 
 College 
 Course 

 
 Ø  College of Nursing 
 BS Medical Technology

 BS Nursing 

 
 Ø  College of Education 
 BS Tourism Management

 BS Hospitality Management 

 
 
   

 Procedure after confirming the slot: 

 
 Physical and Medical procedure is temporarily suspended

 
 
 For those qualified applicants who have already undergone the medical process, please keep your medical results and wait for further announcements.

 
 
 Qualified applicants who have already confirmed their slot will only need to wait for an email from the Office of the University Registrar. The registration process will start on July 27, 2020 . No further action from the applicant is needed upon confirmation of the slot, unless instructed by OSAS or the University Registrar.

 
   

 
 After the Office of the University Registrar has registered the applicant, an email containing the confirmation, and student number along with a TEMPORARY student portal password will be sent to the applicant.

 
 
 For the meantime, requirements submitted to OSAS will be used by the University Registrar. In the future, the University Registrar will ask for the complete original requirements of the confirmed applicants

 
 For inquiries, please send an email to admission@cvsu.edu.ph .

 
 
 
 Concern 
 Subject of the email 

 
 Forgotten the email address used in the registration? Kindly send a copy of your application slip as proof of identity 
 Control number – forgotten email account

 Ex. 99999 – Forgotten email account 

 
 Any misspellings in the name?

 Kindly send a copy of your application slip as proof of identity 
 Control number – Change of Name

 Ex. 99999 – Change of Name

----- DOC | Enrollment for Teacher Certificate Program | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/07/03/enrollment-for-teacher-certificate-program/ | 2020-07-03 -----
The University is now accepting enrollees for the Teacher Education Program (TCP) .

 College of Education – Teacher Education Department is a gateway in pursuing lifelong learning and a partner in nurturing learners for those interested to be professional teachers.

 For initial evaluation, applicants must send a copy of their scanned TOR to ced@cvsu.edu.ph 

 TCP is a non-degree program designed for individuals who have completed any Bachelor's degree in any field who intend to teach at the secondary level.

----- DOC | CvSU GAD Advocates participate in IPC WE CaN seminar | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/07/03/cvsu-gad-advocates-participate-in-ipc-we-can-seminar/ | 2020-07-03 -----
The Institute of Philippine Culture (IPC) together with the Department of Science and Technology – Philippine Council for Health Research and Development (DOST-PCHRD) led a three-hour webinar series entitled “ Ako at ang COVID-19: Ang Nakatagong Pandemya ng Karahasan Laban sa Kababaihan” last June 17, 2020. 

 The session was focused on how the quarantine affected prevalence of domestic violence among Filipino households. It also tackled the current issues and conditions of gender-based violence and cases on violence against women  in the Philippines.

 The activity was jointly participated by 304 different GAD advocates and experts from various units of academic institutions, private sectors, and local government units. In addition, the webinar gave inputs about the launched campaign named Lunas Collective, a feminist, inclusive chat service that runs on the compassion of volunteers — a safe online space where people seeking support related to gender-based violence and reproductive health can expect that they will be heard.

 The founder and one of the speakers, Asst. Prof. Sabrina Laya Gacad from the University of the Philippines, stressed the importance of having a support system that allows the victims and survivors of different gender-based violence to protect and to know their rights. Lunas Collective has since partnered with Likhaan Center for Women's Health in referring clients for reproductive health consultations, and the Psychological Association of the Philippines for training. It also has the staunch support of the University of the Philippines' Center for Women's and Gender Studies and the Department of Women and Development Studies in its initiative.

 
On the other hand, Dr. Nathalie Africa – Verceles, Director for Center for Women's and Gender Studies of University of the Philippines, talked about her expertise with regards to domestic violence among Filipino households and the possible coping mechanisms of the victims and survivors. The webinar was facilitated by Dr. Enrique Leviste, director, Institute of Philippine Culture (IPC ). (KDAgreda) 

 IPC WE Can Seminar Promotional poster

----- DOC | GAD-RC Conducts Refresher Training on MR GAD KATROPA | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/07/03/gad-rc-conducts-refresher-training-on-mr-gad-katropa/ | 2020-07-03 -----
GAD Resource Center, in partnership with Commission on Population and Development Region IV Calabarzon, conducted a Refresher Training on Men's Responsibility on Gender and Development KAlalakihang Tapat sa Responsibilidad at Obligasyon sa PAmilya (MR GAD KATROPA) via Zoom last June 18, 2020 with 10 female and 8 male participants.

 Ms. Raecel A. Estebat, OIC, GAD-RC, discussed the overview of the refresher's course. She pointed out that this was primarily conducted to re-educate and re-qualify GAD Coordinators as MR GAD KATROPA Advocates and that the expected output for this special training will be the series of online trainings/webinars on Responsible Parenthood, Reproductive Health Care and Family Planning to the community as an extension project of the GAD-RC.

 Ms. Marilyn F. Ogaya, Assistant Regional Director – Designate, POPCOM Region IV discussed the Overview of the RP-FP Manual, Nurturing Harmonious Relationships in the Family, and Responsible Parenting. Ms. Ogaya also discussed the safety measures against diseases in line with the COVID-19 pandemic as part of the responsibilities of the home management.

 Ms. Charmaine E. Sarabia, Planning Officer I, POPCOM Region IV discussed the Importance and Benefits of Family Planning and Decision-making for Couples and Family Planning. Ms. Sarabia also discussed the family planning myths and misconceptions.

 

 Participants who attended the activity thru Zoom

----- DOC | GAD-RC Conducts the 2nd Quarter GAD TWG Meeting | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/gad-rc-conducts-the-2nd-quarter-gad-twg-meeting/ | 2020-06-25 -----
GAD Resource Center conducted its 2 nd Quarter GAD TWG Meeting via Zoom on May 26, 2020. The highlight of the meeting was compliance to PCW MC No. 2020-03 which calls for adjustment in the GAD Plan and Budget to be realigned to the mandates of PCW in addressing gender issues brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.

 GAD Coordinators were asked to provide the data on deployed personnel and project/program beneficiaries of the different colleges, campuses, and units of the university during the ECQ. They were also reminded to ensure that GAD is integrated in all instruction, research and extension activities of their respective colleges and campuses.

 Plan of activities of KATROPA and MOVE were also discussed as it was noted that there was an increase in VAWC cases during the ECQ.

----- DOC | CvSU GAD advocates participate in CTA | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/cvsu-gad-advocates-participate-in-cta/ | 2020-06-25 -----
One male and 9 female GAD advocates from different colleges, campuses, and units of Cavite State University attended a webinar session entitled “Conversations through Art (CTA): Tapping Various Creative Modalities for Intervention, Growth, and Transformation” organized by the University of the Philippines Center for Women's and Gender Studies (UPCWGS), in partnership with Art Ventures and Advocacy Network, last May 14, 2020.

 The fifth session discussed topics about Women, Gender, and Film. It celebrated the brilliance and remarkable artistry of Filipino filmmakers, and how their works contribute to changing the world views and perceives women's and gender issues.

 Various lecture presentations were given and facilitated by reputable speakers to promote arts for social change. Film directors Antoninette Jadaone and Sigrid Andrea Bernardo gave lecture on social responsibilities in fighting the high prevalence of gender differences and stereotypes in Philippine Cinema. They have also shared their struggles and success in conquering independent to mainstream films. Filmmaker, Samantha Lee discussed her advocacy for better representation of women and LGBTQ+ community in Philippine Cinema. She had pointed out the importance of closing the gap of gender inequality in Philippine Cinema which is considered to be a powerful community medium representation. The challenges in film binary representation were also tackled during the lecture.

 The three-hour seminar workshop was moderated by Ms. Tez Cruz, founder of Feminist Media Lab (FeMMeLAB). The webinar was attended by 124 participants from various academes, GAD Advocates, Film enthusiasts and Filmmakers. (KDAgreda) 

 Conversations through Art: Women, Gender, and Film Promotional poster

----- DOC | AAs, students receive food packs | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/aas-students-receive-food-packs/ | 2020-06-25 -----

----- DOC | NCRDEC's Warm Mugs launched | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/ncrdecs-warm-mugs-launched/ | 2020-06-25 -----
Recognizing the hardwork of agency-based utility workers, the National Coffee Research, Development and Extension Center (NCRDEC) launched “Warm Mugs”, a relief assistance that called for monetary donation, purchased goods for distribution and delivered food packs to the said target beneficiaries.

 Total collection from sponsors amounting to PhP 40,892.00 were used to buy grocery items that include rice, canned goods and noodles. Meanwhile, those living outside Indang were sent cash assistance considering that they are on a “no work, no pay” basis since the start of the community quarantine. Also included in the beneficiaries are farm workers serving in the University.

 In addition, the sacrifices and hardwork of the law enforcers were also honored through the giving of 100 packs of Aguinaldo Blend coffee to the frontliners in Cavite with the assistance of PMaj. Fernel Dela Cruz of the Philippine National Police. The first 20 packs of Aguinaldo Blend coffee were distributed to quality controlled points managed by the Philippine National Police (PNP) and barangay personnel in the Municipality of Indang last April 13, 2020. The second batch of 65 packs of coffee were given to the Camp BGen Pantaleon Garcia in Imus City and were distributed to the assistant team leaders of checkpoint and selected police stations of the city. The remaining 15 packs of coffee were given to the Office of the Provincial Director, Base Police Personnel, and Provincial Logistics Branch of the Cavite Police Provincial Office.

----- DOC | CCAT's Bayanihan aids community groups | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/ccats-bayanihan-aids-community-groups/ | 2020-06-25 -----
CvSU – CCAT located in Rosario, Cavite has been extending assistance to community members through donations from different individuals and associations like Rotaract Club of CvSU-CCAT, Rotary PEZA and campus faculty and staff.

 PPSS and OSAS Unit provided rice assistance for nine construction workers from Calumpit, Bulacan and neighboring towns of Cavite stranded in the campus due to ECQ. Food packs were also distributed to 180 residents in Rosario and Tanza while the second wave benefited 30 families living within Rosario, Noveleta, Tanza, General Trias and Kawit. Food and drinks were also given to 50 barangay officials and volunteers, 80 frontliners in Divine Grace Medical Hospital, and 96 outpost attendants, street sweepers, and other residents in selected towns. Donations from members of Faculty and Employees Association, MPC and EBA have been converted into 29 sacks of rice for 139 families from the adopted community (Gawad Kalinga) and for security personnel and utility workers. Meanwhile, 335 kilos of rice were given to 103 tricycle drivers of EPTETODA and CvSU TODA. In another relief operation, sets of food and drinks were also shared to 139 families, 103 tricycle drivers and 200 frontliners in checkpoints in Rosario, Tanza and City of General Trias.

 In terms of health and sanitation, a total of 125 hygiene kits were donated to LGU front liners like baragay tanod, police, coast guards and other attendants in checkpoints .  Medical Services Department also provided health and nutrition education, preventive care, prenatal care and vitamin tablets to 19 construction workers and their immediate family members.

 All of these were made possible through the spirit of cooperation and generosity – the Bayanihan in CvSU-CCAT

The College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources provided support to both animals and humans in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

----- DOC | DOST-CvSU's SciCAT sends fresh fruits | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/25/dost-cvsus-scicat-sends-fresh-fruits/ | 2020-06-25 -----
The advantage of having a farm is not only that it can supply a family's needs but it can also share the harvest to others especially during this time of crisis.

 This is what the Science for the Convergence of Agriculture and Tourism (SciCAT) Project Team did this April 2020 in a series of distribution of fresh fruits harvested from Silan Agri Farm.

 The first 188 ripe papaya were distributed to frontliners from the Philippine National Police (PNP) assigned in Barangay 1, staff of the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO), Traffic Enforcers Unit, IATF Operations, and Engineering Office in Barangay 3, and those from the  Rural Health Unit in Barangay 4, Indang, Cavite.

 The second wave has distributed 101 fresh papaya to the frontliners in General Emilio Aguinaldo Memorial (GEAM) Hospital in Trece Martires City, Cavite while the third wave benefitted 185 frontliners in the Public Market, Bureau of Fire Protection and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office in Indang, MV Santiago Medical Center in Trece Martires City, and City of General Trias Doctors Medical Center.

 The fourth and last batch of fresh papaya was then given to a total of 97 frontliners assigned in Brgy. Bancod, Indang and those serving as guards and skeleton staff of Cavite State University.

 Project SciCAT is funded by the Department of Science and Technology- Philippine Council for Agriculture, Aquatic and Natural Resources Research and Development implemented by CvSU in partnership with Agri Silan Farm in Indang.

 Freshly harvested papaya from Silan Agri Farm 
 Frontliners receive papaya from Project SciCAT

 16        TSAI, Kristi Yana Fae N.    

 UP Manila, Degree Program with Available Slots

 17        VICEDO, Cyrene A. 

 UP Baguio, BA Communication

 
 This year, the CvSU Testing Center served as one of the UPCAT testing centers in Region IV-A.

 UPCAT is administered to graduates of Philippine and foreign high schools in various testing centers across the country and held once a year as part of the admission requirements of the University of the Philippines with more than 100,000 applicants. The test consists of four subtests: Language Proficiency (in English and Filipino), Reading Comprehension (in English and Filipino), Science, and Mathematics. University of the Philippines is known for its strong commitment to academic excellence and highly competitive admission process. (LCosta/YMarinay)

----- DOC | GAD-RC facilitates 1st online meeting | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/22/gad-rc-facilitates-1st-online-meeting/ | 2020-06-22 -----
The GAD Resource Center conducted its 1 st office meeting via Zoom on April 30, 2020. Among the topics discussed were the center's plans and programs in relation to the Philippine Commission on Women's Memorandum Circular directing all government agencies to adjust and/or implement their approved 2020 GAD Plan and Budget in lieu of the current COVID19 pandemic. Considering the work-from-home scheme implemented by the University, the tasking of each of the GAD-RC staff was also discussed to ensure continuity of the center's day to day activities.  Details on the data to be collected to support the GAD programs and projects were also tackled to ensure that all activities are properly documented and monitored.

----- DOC | GAD-RC conducts Orientation with Federation of Persons with Disability – Cavite, Inc. | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/22/gad-rc-conducts-orientation-with-federation-of-persons-with-disability-cavite-inc/ | 2020-06-22 -----
With the theme “Time for Change: Realizing the Rights of Women with Disabilities”, Federation of Persons with Disability – Cavite, Inc (FPDCI) and GAD-RC, conducted an Orientation on Gender Sensitivity last March 11, 2020 at CACODEC Seminar Area, Provincial Capitol Compound, Trece Martires City, Cavite.

 Ms. Raecel A. Estebat, OIC of GADRC, discussed the difference between sex and gender, gender role, gender stereotyping and agents of gender socialization. She emphasized that both culture and society have a very big impact on the expectation on the characteristics, aptitudes, and likely behaviors of both women and men. She also added that gender socialization begins with the family as soon as the baby is  born.

 Mr. David D. Gonzaga, President, FPDCI, expressed his gratitude to CvSU in providing the orientation to their members.

 The said activity is also in line with the celebration of Women's Month and the 16 th Women with Disabilities Day. It was attended by 7 female and 14 male participants.

----- DOC | CvSU GAD-RC attends the 4th Regional Women's Month Celebration | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/22/cvsu-gad-rc-attends-the-4th-regional-womens-month-celebration/ | 2020-06-22 -----
Gender and Development Resource Center attended the CALABARZON Regional Development Council's 4 th Regional Women's Month Celebration conducted last March 5, 2020 at the University of Rizal System, Morong, Rizal.

 In the said activity made possible through the Regional Gender and Development Committee, USec. Carim Panumpang of the Department of Agrarian Reform highlighted the efforts of DAR in mainstreaming gender and development and ensuring the inclusion of rural women in its services.

 A film on teenage pregnancy produced by the Commission on Population was also shown during the program. The film showing was intended to increase the awareness of the participants on the growing issue of teenage pregnancy in the region. Also included in the program is the presentation from a personnel from DAR and cultural presentations of the students from URS.

 Meanwhile, DOH Region IV-A provided free pneumonia vaccine to the participants. while haircut, massage, manicure, and pedicure was also provided by TESDA Rizal.

 Dir. Rene Colocar of DAR Region IV-A, requested the participants to join the singing of the song entitled, “Juana”, which was prepared by the Cavite State University.

----- DOC | Consultative Meeting with IAPWDI held | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/22/consultative-meeting-with-iapwdi-held/ | 2020-06-22 -----
A consultative meeting with nine (9) female and five (5) male officers of the Indang Association of Persons with Disability, Inc. (IAPWDI) was held as part of the continuous extension program of the Gender and Development Resource Center in its  Empowerment of Gender and Development Stakeholders for Achieving Gender and Equality in Calabarzon.

 Ms. Raecel A. Estebat, OIC, GAD-RC, discussed the 2020 Calendar of Activities to the officers of the association. Topics given include: Gender-Responsive Disaster; Preparedness; Basic GAD Concepts; Magna Carta of Women; Magna Carta for PWD; Anti-Rape Law; Orientation on SOGIE; and Gender-Fair Language.

 Among the given topics, the officers of the association identified the seminar on disaster risk preparedness as an immediate concern of the group. This was  due to numerous reports they have received that PWDs were left in their houses during the evacuation caused by the eruption of the Taal volcano.

----- DOC | GAD-RC conducts Gender Sensitivity Seminar among CCJ students | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/22/gad-rc-conducts-gender-sensitivity-seminar-among-ccj-students/ | 2020-06-22 -----
In preparation for their On-the-Job Training (OJT), GAD Resource Center (GAD-RC) conducted an orientation on gender sensitivity and sexual harassment among 9 female and 21 male  students of the College of Criminal Justice last February 3, 2020.

 

 Ms. Raecel A. Estebat, OIC, GAD-RC, discussed Gender Sensitivity explaining the Difference between Sex and Gender, Gender Role, Gender Stereotyping and Agents of Gender Socialization. Different activities were done by the students for them to fully understand the topics discussed. A video about the difference between sex and gender was also shown to the participants. They were also asked to comment and identify the gender issues on the video.

 Written exercises were also given to the participants after the discussion on the use of gender-fair language.

 The second speaker, Mr. John Renz D. Emelo, staff, GAD-RC, discussed the Anti-Sexual Harassment Act of 1995. Procedures on how to report sexual harassment were also explained to the OJTs.

 The activity ended with the participants sharing their insights on the topics discussed.

----- DOC | Announcement for Admission Main Campus | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/06/09/announcement-for-admission-main-campus/ | 2020-06-09 -----
Admission examination of applicants for incoming first year college, transferees and second coursers is cancelled due to COVID-19 pandemic.

 
 
 
 Applicants who had a schedule of appointment with the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) from March 11-30, 2020 and those who registered online from April 1-10, 2020 may submit their credentials in any available means including but not limited to the following:

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Electronic mail (Email)

 

 

 
 cvsuosasmain@cvsu.edu.ph 

 
 
 
 
 
 Google form (link will be sent by OSAS Personnel thru email)

 FB messenger

 

 

 
 Cavite State University Main OSAS

 
 
 
 
 
 Mail, parcel, courier, etc.

 

 

 
 Office of Student Affairs and Services
Cavite State University
Indang, Cavite 
 
 
 
 
 
 Drop box at Indang Municipal Office

 Drop box at CvSU-Main Campus Entrance Gates (Gate 1 & Gate 2)

 

 

 
 
 
 The following are the required documents/credentials which can be submitted either in hard or soft copy (scanned, screen shots etc.):

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Copy of the filled out application form for admission;

 Grade 12 report card; and

 Certification of good moral character

 

 

 
  

 
 The grades of applicants shall be evaluated according to the admission policy of the University. Below are the documents to be submitted for evaluation:

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Incoming 1 st year –           Grade 12 report card of Senior High School graduates/completers

 Transferees  –           Transcript of records

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Second coursers  –           Transcript of records

 

 

 
 
 
 The grades reflected in the Transcript of Record (TOR) / Certificate of Grades (COG) of transferees and second coursers will be evaluated in accordance with the admission/retention policy of CvSU. The grade requirement for transferee is GPA of 2.00 (equivalent of 85%) or better and no failed grade.

 
 
 
 
 No transferee or shiftee shall be admitted for the following programs and year level:

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Engineering programs – after the first year level

 Doctor of Veterinary Medicine program – after the fourth year level

 Other programs – after the second year level

 

 

 
 
 
 
 Application for transferees and second course students is still open . They may apply on-line using this link: https://registrar.cvsu.edu.ph/osas/ and may submit their application form and other requirements in any available means including but not limited to the following:

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Electronic mail (Email)

 

 

 
 cvsuosasmain@cvsu.edu.ph 

 
 
 
 
 
 Google form (link will be sent by OSAS Personnel thru email)

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 FB messenger

 

 

 
 Cavite State University Main OSAS

 
 
 
 
 
 Mail, parcel, courier, etc.

 

 

 
 Office of Student Affairs and Services
Cavite State University
Indang, Cavite 
 
 
 
 Qualified applicants for college admission shall be notified regarding their admission in any means, including but not limited to email, FB Messenger, text messages, phone calls, courier, etc.

 
 
 
 
 Application for admission of incoming first year college students at CvSU Main Campus is already closed . The Main campus has reached more than the maximum capacity/number of applicants for First Semester, SY 2020- 2021.

----- DOC | TRAFFIC ADVISORY NO. VPASS -01 s 2020 | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/05/traffic-advisory-no-vpass-01-s-2020/ | 2020-05-17 -----
TRAFFIC ADVISORY NO. VPASS -01 s 2020

 EFFECTIVE MAY 18, 2020, CAVITE STATE UNIVERSITY MAIN CAMPUS WILL IMPLEMENT A NEW TRAFFIC SCHEME INSIDE THE UNIVERSITY.

 1. The University is not yet on a “Business As Usual” status because of the current situation of the province under GCQ. Only OFFICIALS, FACULTY MEMBERS AND EMPLOYEES are allowed to enter the University. Visitors, students, parents, and guardians are not allowed.

 2. The ATM and the GSIS Kiosk is temporarily unavailable for public use due to connection problem.

 3. Entrance to the Campus shall be through GATE 1 and exit will be through GATE 2. There will be a ONE-WAY TRAFFICE SCHEME in the major road of the Campus.

 4. All vehicles coming from Kaytapos shall enter through the RIGHT lane of Gate 1 while all vehicles coming from Poblacion and Bancod shall enter through the LEFT lane of Gate 1. Strictly, there will only be two lanes of vehicles entering the campus to facilitate checking of requirements and thermal scanning.

 5. The road from Admin to Gate 1 is closed for traffic. Exit will be at Gate 2. All employees assigned at the SELS, HEVTED and CEd shall enter through the entrance in front of CDC.

 6. SOCIAL DISTANCING SHALL BE STRICTLY OBSERVED. All vehicles carrying passengers more than the required sacrificing social distancing shall not be allowed access.

 7. All passengers and drivers of the vehicles entering the University shall pass through thermal scanning. All vehicles with passengers exhibiting symptoms of cough and colds or with temperature above normal (37.5 oC) shall not be allowed access.

 8. Everyone is highly enjoined to cooperate to avoid traffic jam.

----- DOC | Implementing Guidelines For All Individuals Who Will Enter The University Premises | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/05/implementing-guidelines-for-all-individuals-who-will-enter-the-university-premises/ | 2020-05-17 -----
PUBLIC ADVISORY NO. OVPASS-1- s 2020

 IMPLEMENTING GUIDELINES FOR ALL INDIVIDUALS WHO WILL ENTER THE UNIVERSITY PREMISES (ALL CAMPUSES)

 Effective May 18, 2020

 I. STRICT IMPLEMENTATION OF THE DOH, IATF, and POST ECQ PROTOCOLS for every individual who will enter inside the University premises (all campuses)

 a. Outsiders and employees who are identified as PUIs or PUMs are not allowed to enter the University premises without clearance from DOH. The negative result of the COVID-19 test or the Updated Health Monitoring Card must be presented. Employees who are returning from other municipalities/provinces/regions with several COVID-19 cases shall also seek clearance from RHU.

 b. All individuals entering the University premises are required to wear a face mask. No Face Mask, No Entry Policy shall be implemented in the University.

 c. The security guards who will man the University Gates shall be provided with the required PPEs and shall be equipped with thermal scanners.

 d. Security guards with scanner will be posted at the gate of the campus to check all the requirements and to monitor body temperature of all individuals who wish to enter the University premises.

 e. All individuals found to have temperature above the normal (37.5 oC or higher), or who have cough or colds, will not be given access to the campus.

 f. The use of biometrics to check the attendance of the employees is temporarily suspended. The use of logbooks is encouraged.

 g. Roving SGs are instructed to apprehend all individuals who will not observe proper physical distancing. Likewise, those gathering in a group of more than 10 people without observing proper distancing shall be dispersed.

 h. All deliveries are not allowed access inside the University, except those that are official and authorized by the University President.

 II. ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS FOR FACULTY MEMBERS, EMPLOYEES AND STUDENTS

a. No Employee ID, No Entry policy will be implemented. Faculty members and employees who seek entrance to the campus shall present their valid employee IDs as required in the checkpoints. Their names shall be submitted to the security guards on duty and shall be identified employees authorized to report to the campus.

 b. Faculty members and employees who are not on the list shall not be allowed access to the University.

 c. All reporting employees shall bring their own food and utensils. No canteen will be allowed to open for dine in.

 d. Since the semester officially ended, no students are allowed entrance to the University except those who are given special permission by the campus administrators or college deans.

----- DOC | Fresh papaya from Silan farm distributed | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/fresh-papaya-from-silan-farm-distributed/ | 2020-04-28 -----
Through the Science for the Convergence of Agriculture and Tourism (SciCAT) Project, Cavite State University, in partnership with Silan farm has distributed fresh papaya to frontliners assigned in various areas in Cavite.

 For the three waves of distribution, more than 300 fresh papaya were delivered to the Public Market,  Bureau of Fire Protection, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and Local Government Unit in Indang, General Emilio Aguinaldo Memorial Hospital and MV Santiago Medical Center in Trece Martires City, and City of General Trias Doctors Medical Center in the City of General Trias.

 SciCAT is funded by the Department of Science and Technology – Philippine Council Agriculture, Aquatic and Natural Resources Research and Development. The said project is currently being led by Dr. Lilibeth P. Novicio, Director of Extension Services.

----- DOC | CvSU unites to cheer frontliners through poems | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/cvsu-unites-to-cheer-frontliners-through-poems/ | 2020-04-28 -----
In line with the celebration of April as the National Month of Literature, Cavite State University launched “Bayanihan sa pamamagitan ng Panitikan”, a call for the members of the community to share poems of inspiration to motivate readers and uplift their spirits amidst the crisis.

 Dr. Hernando D. Robles, University President, started expressing his support to the frontliners with his piece entitled “Bagong Bayani: Salamat Frontliners”. Lising, Director of International and Local Collaboration and Linkages Office.

 Featured pieces from students include: “Bayani” by Eldrian Contreras (Bachelor in Elementary Education); “Corona o Korona” by Via Mae Villanueva (Bachelor in Secondary Education); “Ang Nakaraang Kasalukuyan” by Mark Noriel Lacbayen (BS Business Management); “Mga Bayani ng Pandemya” by Vincent Olipano (BS Business Management); “Frontliners” by Jasper Famini (Bachelor of Secondary Education); and “Laban Frontliners” by Jackie Palpallatoc (BS Education major in Mathematics).

----- DOC | CEd shares healthy, innovative recipes | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/ced-shares-healthy-innovative-recipes/ | 2020-04-28 -----
Aside from social distancing and following the rules of quarantine, boosting the immune system and keeping a healthy diet is also necessary to fight COVID-19.

----- DOC | CvSU Cavite City helps communities amidst crisis | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/cvsu-cavite-city-helps-communities-amidst-crisis/ | 2020-04-27 -----
In response to COVID-19, CvSU-Cavite City faculty and staff extended kindness to over 400 households, including fisherfolks of Barangay 61-A Talong, Cavite City.

 Aside from donations for the frontliners at the Philippine General Hospital, the campus' NSTP program spearheaded the distribution of food to those on duty at several checkpoints in Cavite City and Noveleta.

 The Department of Management, in collaboration with the Junior Marketing Association of Cavite City, gave food packs and cash donations to selected students and their families. It also partnered with Garita Elementary School in distributing food to the barangay officials of Brgy. 55-Ampalaya.

 Meanwhile, faculty members of the Departments of Information and Technology, Teacher Education and Languages, and Arts and Sciences, were also active in assisting their respective communities.

 Campus Administrator Ms. Maria Cristina J. Baesa who personally donated homemade disinfectants to 245 households in several barangays, emphasized that this is an opportunity for CvSU family to practically embody the virtue of service being considered as one of the insitution's core values.

 “ Ang inyo pong handog ay napakalaking tulong po sa aming mga napiling residente. Maraming marami pong salamat sa mga sponsors ninyo. Huwag po sana kayong magsasawang tumulong ,” one of the beneficiaries said while expressing gratitude for the assistance received. (JJangad)

----- DOC | CvSU Bacoor implements four projects against COVID-19 | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/cvsu-bacoor-implements-four-projects-against-covid-19/ | 2020-04-27 -----
The pandemic that has gripped the country in the past months has not only changed the way we look at things and at people, but also the manner we respond to unexpected circumstances. We may think and act differently but at the end of the day, we will realize that we would rather do something that matters, something that can make a difference in other people's lives, however small that act may be. This has inspired the whole community of Cavite State University Bacoor City Campus to help ease the struggles and hardships individuals and families experience as they face the crisis that is COVID-19.

 The campus has successfully implemented four projects in March as it tried to combat the spread of coronavirus and mitigate its effect on the lives of the most vulnerable groups within the community.

 More than 200 copies of flyers and leaflets of an infographic about cleaning and disinfecting using chlorine/water and saline solutions were distributed to nearby establishments and residences. CvSU Bacoor employees and students helped in the dissemination to the general public through social media, particularly via Facebook.

 Relief packs were also provided to 17 stranded students residing near the campus assuring the students that they are not alone in their battle against COVID-19. A free layout/photo editing service was also offered to the 65 members of the Barangay Mambog IV COVID-19 quarantine team for the production of their IDs. Finally, 16 sets of Zonrox-water solutions and salt were distributed to selected residents in Perpetual Village 6 in Mambog IV.

 They say that the most beautiful things in life are not seen but felt by the heart. But if we can see the joy in the eyes of those who have received our small acts of kindness, then happiness takes on a different meaning. We fight COVID-19 as one. We heal as one. (MLLamanilao) 

 Store attendant receives IEC material from CvSU Bacoor

----- DOC | CvSU sends RELIEF against COVID-19 | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/04/cvsu-sends-relief-against-covid-19/ | 2020-04-27 -----
Even with the implementation of the enhanced community quarantine, Cavite State University extended service to its stakeholders with the banner program “CvSU RELIEF: Kontra COVID-19, implemented through its 12 campuses in Cavite.

 As of April 9, 2020, it has delivered 1,935 food packs, including freshly harvested fish and vegetables to students stranded in dormitories, employees who serve as frontliners like security personnel and utility workers, and community members who live near the campuses. Free transportation service was also offered to students who were affected by the lockdown and are now reunited with their families.

 For medical supplies, CvSU has donated more than 4, 611 face masks and face shields including materials used in the production of such to hospitals in the province and checkpoints where frontliners have been assigned. It has also distributed 12,047 health and sanitation kits that include vitamins, alcohol, soap and disinfectants to community members.

 In battling against fake information spreading over the net, CvSU released around 73 information materials that explain facts about COVID-19, social distancing, use of masks, and disinfection among others. It also provided links for credible sites and online courses that may be studied to increase productivity during the quarantine. Healthy recipes were also published encouraging creativity even with simple ingredients like sardines and camote.

 Various campuses also offer psychosocial support programs like online counseling to address the anxiety that may be brought by the crisis. It has also been circulating prayers and spiritual support to encourage people to pray for the country. Through its Facebook page, it also releases poems of hope written by its students and employees spreading words of motivation for the readers.

 Hundreds of its alumni are also serving as frontliners in the field of health, security, banking and finance, social work and other jobs necessary to keep the nation moving.

 With cash donations from various individuals and in coordination with partner agencies, CvSU continues to deliver assistance to those in need while hoping that this crisis will soon end.

 RELIEF stands for Research and Extension Life-changing Initiatives for the Empowerment of Filipinos that was first launched in December 2019 as it sent donations to earthquake victims in Cotabato, relaunched in January as aid to families affected by the Taal eruption and is now being implemented to help fight against COVID-19.

 Reunited with her family through free transportation service 
 Students receive freshly harvested fish from the Techno-Demo Farm 
 Students in boarding houses receive food packs weekly 
 Homemade disinfectants were distributed

 Update Advisory as of March 17, 2020 
 CvSU sends RELIEF against COVID-19

----- DOC | Update Advisory as of March 17, 2020 | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/03/update-advisory-as-of-march-17-2020/ | 2020-03-17 -----
In response to the declaration of the President placing entire Luzon into Enhanced Community Quarantine, the following shall be strictly followed: 

 1. All classes in all levels at the Main Campus, the satellite campuses, and all learning centers in Cavite, QNAS, WPU, MinSCAT, OMSC and MSC will remain suspended until April 14, 2020. Coordinators from the said LC shall coordinate with the OVPAA for guidance on the use of alternative modes of instruction during the period. Likewise, GS in-charge in the external LC shall inform their counterparts with regards to class suspension. 

 2. Faculty and employees are allowed to work from home. Classes can be in any form of alternative modes approved by the Academic Council such as, but not limited to, online classes, group chats, Google Classroom, etc. 

 3. Non-academic employees are not required to report for work except frontliners such as selected PPS personnel who will do disinfecting works, security guards, skeletal force who will feed animals and water plants, workers in the water refilling station and other research projects that need time to time attention, and medical personnel to extend assistance to RHU, MHU and other health providers. However, social distancing for those selected to form the skeletal force shall be observed. 

 4. The whole University and its campuses are close for outside visitors during the quarantine period. 

 5. The Management of the private companies providing services to the University shall take charge of their own employees (Janitorial and Security Services) with strict compliance with enhanced quarantine guidelines. 

 6. All other employees not involved in any skeletal work force shall stay at home and shall use only electronic means for any announcement, queries and advisories. 

 7. The University will not allow any request for use of any University facilities such as venue for sports event, conferences, wedding and reception venues, birthdays, pre-nup photo shoot and all other activities during the community quarantine period. Hostel Tropicana and International House will be closed for Balikbayan Tourists and other guests during the said period. 

 8. These guidelines will be updated from time to time as necessary. 

 9. Your cooperation is highly enjoined. 

 10. Thank you.

----- DOC | Additional Guidelines on the Prevention of COVID-19 Transmission | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/03/additional-guidelines-on-the-prevention-of-covid-19-transmission/ | 2020-03-16 -----
ADDITIONAL GUIDELINES ON THE PREVENTION OF COVID-19 TRANSMISSION THROUGH SOCIAL DISTANCING AND FOR ALL OTHER PURPOSES IN ITS MANAGEMENT

 1. In addition to initial guidelines issued on March 15, 2020 by the OUP through PACO, the following additional guidelines shall be strictly followed:

 1.1 All classes, both graduate and undergraduate, will remain suspended until April 14, 2020 or until further notice.

 1.2 No students shall be allowed to enter the premises of the university including in the branch/satellite campuses during the period of class suspension except on the following cases:

 1.2.1 Those students who are conducting thesis inside campus which involved plants and animals that need to be watered and fed. However, they shall be strictly monitored and advised to get out immediately after their task for the day is completed. Prolong stay in the vicinity is prohibited.

 1.3 Outside visitors shall not be allowed during the period where classes are suspended and they should be advised that any transaction shall be by electronic means (text messages, messenger, e-mail, etc.).

 1.4 The University Mall and other concessionaires are hereby advised to temporarily close operation from March 16-April 14, 2020 when there are no students except the following:

 1.4.1 Those selling food items/groceries and other day-to-day public necessities such as alcohol, tissue papers, soap, etc. However, they should limit the number of customers getting inside the stores or devise a strategy how to lessen customers queue in any given time. During the period from March 16 to April 14, those who will voluntarily close their shops will not be required to pay rentals.

 1.5 VP for Academic Affairs shall submit to OUP for approval the proposal/plans to fulfill students educational requirements during the said period.

 1.6 VPASS, VPRE and VPPD shall submit proposals to OUP for approval the measures to implement provision of MC No. 7, s.2020 and CSC Announcement No. 12, s.2020, dated March 16, 2020 re: Alternative Work Arrangement in the Light of Code Red Sub-Level 2 issued by the Office of the President through DOH.

 1.7 Any work arrangement for both faculty and non-teaching personnel that may be approved shall not compromise the effective delivery of service and shall not affect the university productivity and performance with regard to Instruction, Research and Extension and General Services.

 1.8 The University Medical staff is hereby directed to extend assistance to all employees who will be declared as PUI or PUM due to possible exposure during their previous travel to areas with confirmed COVID-19 cases before March 16, 2020. They should coordinate from time to time with the Provincial Health Unit, MHU and DOH Regional office for more reliable information regarding the disease.

 1.9 All official travels (planned or unplanned) outside the province is hereby temporarily suspended.

 1.10 All PPS drivers are hereby instructed to disinfect their respective assigned vehicle and shall remain parked in the motor pool.

 2. Additional guidelines anent to this shall be issued from time to time if the situation warrants.

 3. The cooperation of everyone during the challenging time is highly enjoined.

 4. Please be guided accordingly.

 5. Be safe everyone

----- DOC | Initial Guidelines to Implement Social Distancing | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/03/initial-guidelines-to-implement-social-distancing/ | 2020-03-15 -----
INITIAL GUIDELINES TO IMPLEMENT SOCIAL DISTANCING EFFECTIVE MARCH 16-17,2020

 
 All faculty members including JO part time are not required to report for work on March 16-17 until further notice. Non-academic employees, however, are required to report but should exercise social distance of at least 1 meter .

 Avoid congregating anywhere within the University including campuses.

 No students or student applicants shall be allowed entry until further advise.

 Offices shall not accept outside visitors for the mean time.

 All employees are encouraged to bring own food or take lunch at home. Eating at canteen restaurants is discouraged.

 Important messages, queries, advisories shall be by electronics means.

 PPS Staff, SGs, and Outsource Janitorial Staff are directed to do disinfecting works in all buildings.

 Additional updates will be issued after the meeting with the LGU and other agencies involved in the COVID-19 management.

 All employees are discouraged to post, like , share any unofficial release not coming from WHO, DOH, DILG , IATF for COVID-19 and concerned LGUs.

 Please be guided accordingly

 Be safe everyone.

 
 Ref: CMO 19, 2005, CHED Advisory Nos. 1-4, s. 2020. IATF Resolution 11-12. OPG Advisories.

----- DOC | CvSU Imus receives AYA 2019-2020 from PMA | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/03/cvsu-imus-receives-aya-2019-2020-from-pma/ | 2020-03-12 -----
Cavite State University Imus Campus bagged Agora Youth Award (AYA) 2019-2020 for being one of the Marketing Schools of the Year recognized by the Philippine Marketing Association (PMA) at the Winford Hotel Manila last March 8.

 The award was given after top executives from prestigious companies served as evaluators of the marketing plans of competing schools in a final judging conducted at Ateneo Professional School, Rockwell, Makati on March 6. The winning proposal entitled “Real-Life Business Case for Toyota Motors Corporation's New Hybrid Electric Vehicle” was presented by seven Business Management major in Marketing students namely Gheanne Mherl Abubo, Leo Basal, Ramonchito Castillo, Maria Victoria Orro Harder, Harlene Ramirez, Aldren Sarmiento, and Leslie Suyat led by their adviser, Ms. Ruby-Lyn T. De Grano and endorsed by Management Department Chairperson Dr. Alfe M. Solina.

 Since its establishment in 1954, Philippine Marketing Association (PMA) remains to be a leading institution for marketing excellence in the country. This year, they collaborated with Toyota Motors Corporation aiming to recognize remarkable companies and individuals who have successfully navigated the evolving business landscape.

  “ I am very proud of the students' achievement. With the 16 schools who competed, it was very overwhelming that Cavite State University Imus Campus made it to the top finalists. During the preparation for the competition, the team took it seriously to perfect their marketing plan, and their hard work paid off ” De Grano said.

 In his Facebook post, Castillo stated that it is an honor to bring the name of CvSU in the said event proving that it can be at par with other universities in the country. ( RL De Grano) 

 The Top 5 finalists: Cavite State University Imus Campus, Miriam College, San Beda University, University of the Philippines Visayas, and University of Santo Tomas 
 CvSU Imus with the keynote speaker, Ma. Cristina Fe Arevalo from Toyota Motors Philippines 
 

 CvSU Imus with current Board of Directors of PMA headed by President, Ms. Faith Abano (extreme right) and the PMA Director for Youth and Academe, Ms. Jaana Abrogena (second to the left)

----- DOC | Senior High students complete immersion at CAFENR | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/03/senior-high-students-complete-immersion-at-cafenr/ | 2020-03-12 -----
Ten senior high school students from Trece Martires City Senior High School and two from Indang Christian Academy completed their work immersion in agriculture at the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources (CAFENR) on February 12, 2020. The immersion is a requirement for senior high school students under the STEM strand.

 From January 28 to February 11, 2020, the students had immersion activities in the following offices: Pollution Control Office, Institute of Food Science and Technology, Environmental Geo-Information and Remote Sensing (EnGIRS) Laboratory, University Library, Sugar Palm Research, Information and Trade Center (SPRINT), Department of Animal Science, Department of Agricultural Entrepreneurship, National Coffee Research, Development, and Extension Center (NCRDEC), Vegetable Production Area, Department of Forestry and Environmental Science, Department of Crop Science, Fruit Crop Nursery, Ornamental Nursery, Herbs Project, Agromet Station, Research Center, Extension Services Office, and CEIT Department of Agricultural and Food Engineering.

 It is CAFENR's second year to accept immersion students as part of implementing its CHED Institutional Development and Innovation Grants (IDIG) on “Reinvigorating the BS Agriculture Curriculum: Developing Agri-technopreneurs”. The activity is also an opportunity for the College to promote its curricular programs and other related courses, such as agricultural engineering, among senior high school students in the province. (AD Cero) 

 Senior high school students holding their certificates of completion

----- DOC | CRVA's 3rd workshop conducted | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/02/crvas-3rd-workshop-conducted/ | 2020-02-27 -----
Representatives from the provincial and municipal agriculturist offices of Cavite, Laguna, Batangas, and Rizal attended the Adaptive Capacity Consultation Workshop at Hostel Tropicana on February 18-19, 2020.

 The activity is the third among the series of workshops being conducted for the Climate Risk Vulnerability Assessment (CRVA) study of the said provinces, which is a collaborative project with the Department of Agriculture Regional Field Office IVA. CRVA is under the umbrella of the Department of Agriculture project “Adaptation and Mitigation Initiative in Agriculture” (AMIA), which aims to establish climate-resilient agri-fisheries communities and build appropriate climate responsive financial and other support services.

 The project is headed by Dr. David L. Cero, dean of the College of Engineering and Information Technology (CEIT), and composed of members from the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources (CAFENR): Dr. Analita M. Magsino, Ms. Jo-an T. Ducducan, and Ms. Amyel Dale L. Cero.

 Consultants from the Geographic Innovations for Development Solutions, Inc. (GRIDS), headed by Mr. Milben Bragais, served as the resource persons of the workshop. (ADCero) 

 Participants of the CRVA Workshop

----- DOC | VP Mojica wins MWF Prize for Eng'g Excellence | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/02/vp-mojica-wins-mwf-prize-for-engg-excellence/ | 2020-02-04 -----
In recognition of his invention Bravura Coffee Roasting Machine, Engr. Ruel M. Mojica, Vice President for Research and Extension, was one of the four awarded in this year's Manila Water Foundation (MWF) Prize for Engineering Excellence during the Awarding Ceremony held at Seda Vertis North Hotel, Quezon City, February 3.

 The said competition organized by MWF recognizes notable inventions of engineers in the country, particularly those that have shown impact in uplifting the lives of the Filipinos. Bravura, known to be the cheapest vertical coffee roaster in the market, provided opportunities for farmers and cooperatives to fully optimize profit by selling roasted coffee instead of raw beans. The said machine was also built through participatory approach, considering farmers' capacity and cost limitations.

 “ Ang isang excellent engineer para sa akin ay may puso na nagmamahal para sa kababayan at may pusong handang tumulong para sa mga kababayan,” Engr. Mojica shares in a video presented during the awarding.

 Other awardees include Engr. Francis Aldrin Uy for the Universal Structural Health Evaluation and Recording System, Engr. Alexis Belonio for the Rice Husk Gassifier Technology and Dr. Michael Gragasin for the Compact Corn Mill and Impeller Rice Mill.

 Dr. Ruel Mojica receives the award together with three other engineers and MWF representatives (Photo from: Ms. Michelle Uy)

----- DOC | CvSU Supports 18-day Campaign to End VAW | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/01/cvsu-supports-18-day-campaign-to-end-vaw/ | 2020-01-28 -----
CvSU Gender and Development Resource Center conducted the 18-Day Campaign to End Violence Against Women on December 9, 2019 at the International Convention Center, CvSU, Indang, Cavite.

 

 The activity was attended by 338 female and 330 male faculty members, employees, students, and stakeholders of different colleges and campuses of the university.

 Mr. Dionisio B. Dalusong, Chief of ther Institutional Development Division of DSWD Field Office IV-A, discussed the roles of men in achieving gender equality and the vision, mission, and objectives of Men Opposed to Violence Against Women Everywhere (MOVE). He also challenged and encouraged the participants to actively participate in the fight against violence against women.

 

 USec. Astravel P. Naik, Executive Director of Commission on Filipinos Overseas, discussed the mandates of their agency and  shared some cases they handled related to human trafficking.

 

 Mr. Dalusong also discussed the Gender-Based Violence (GBV) by pointing that GBV is a human rights violation and is punishable by law.

 Dr. Leyma L. Cero, VP for Planning and Development, and Dr. Julio G. Alava, VP for External and Business Affairs, stated their commitment to end violence against women.

 

 Recognition of partners of the GAD Resource Center, awarding of Certificates of the Winners of the Digital Poster Making, distribution of IEC materials, and promotion of the livelihood projects of the GAD-RC were also done during the activity.

----- DOC | CvSU Participates in the Provincial Women's Summit | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/01/cvsu-participates-in-the-provincial-womens-summit/ | 2020-01-28 -----
CvSU Gender and Development Resource Center staff participated in the Provincial Women's Summit conducted by The Provincial Government of Cavite through its Cavite Gender and Development Focal Point System (CGFPS) on November 25, 2019 at Provincial Gymnasium, Trece Martires City, Cavite.

 

 The activity was attended by women's organizations, Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps) Benificfiaries, LGUs, barangay health workers, uniformed personnel of PNP, the academe and employees of the provincial government.

 In his opening message, Mr. Alvin Mojica, Provincial Administrator for Community Affairs, mentioned that he hopes that the event will be fruitful. Meanwhile, Hon. Irene Bencito, Executive Assistant and former Board Member recognized the efforts done by the Provincial Social Welfare and Development Office of the Cavite GAD Focal Point System in facilitating the activity. She also stressed that the fight of women's right is also a fight of men since it protects the rights of their mothers, wives, sisters, and daughters.

 

 Hon. Lorna Sarabia Toledo, Tagaytay City Councilor, delivered the message of Mayor Agnes Tolentino emphasizing the important roles and influence of women in the development and the impact of this kind of opportunity to empowered women.

 Topics discussed during the summit were: Provincial Gender and Development Code by Ms. Mylene Sotolombo, Local Legislative staff Officer II; Alternative Learning System (ALS): An Instrument for Providing Lifelong Skills by Ms. Felisa C. Matel, Education Program Specialist; Magna Carta of Women by PLTCOL Atty. Arvie Paraon-Bueno of Cavite Provincial Police Office; and Programs provided by TESDA by Ms. Jenelyn Dino.

 Dr. Susan Tan, GAD Chair for Extension of CvSU, facilitated the Talk Show with the speakers. Questions raised deal with the best practices on mainstreaming GAD in the PNP, the ration of male and female beneficiaries of ALS, and the success rate of trainer in TESDA.

 

 Except for the additional information about the women's rights and opportunities, the summit also pampered the participants by providing beauty and wellness services and medical and dental check up.

----- DOC | GAD Staff Trained on MOV Preparation for Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/01/gad-staff-trained-on-mov-preparation-for-gender-mainstreaming-evaluation-framework/ | 2020-01-28 -----
Ms. Raecel A. Estebat, in-charge of GAD Resource Center (GADRC) and Ms. Shirlyn M. The activity was sponsored by the Regional Gender and Development Committee in partnership with Gender and Development Educators and Advocates (AGADe) Co.

Chan, a Certified Member of the National GAD Resource Pool of the Philippine Commission on Women, discussed GAD Mainstreaming as a Strategy to Implement the Magna Carta of Women; Entry Points of Gender Mainstreaming; Enhanced Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework; GAD Mainstreaming Levels; GMEF Scoring Guide and Sheet; Guidelines in Administering the GMEF; and GMEF Descriptors Means of Verifications.

 Meanwhile, Ms. Maria Theresa Hernandez of Batangas State University discussed the Application of GMEF for Higher Education Institutions. She shared the BatSU experience in administering GMEF in their university.

 The second day was devoted in administering GMEF by different agencies/institutions. Each agency/institution presented the GMEF tool assessment based on the four entry points to gender mainstreaming.

----- DOC | CvSU GAD Agenda Reviewed and Revised | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/01/cvsu-gad-agenda-reviewed-and-revised/ | 2020-01-28 -----

----- DOC | GADRC Facilitates Gender Sensitivity Seminar for Parents of Day Care Students | https://cvsu.edu.ph/2020/01/gadrc-facilitates-gender-sensitivity-seminar-for-parents-of-day-care-students/ | 2020-01-28 -----
Parents of Day Care students of Brgy. Lallana, Trece Martires City, Cavite participated in a Gender Sensitivity Seminar last June 20, 2019 held at the Covered Court of the said barangay. The activity is part of the Memorandum of Agreement between the University and Brgy. Lallana.

 Ms. Shirlyn M. Rom-Leachon started with the objectives of Gender and Development as well as the services offered by the university. She enumerated the differences between sex and gender and involved the participants by asking them of the things only a woman can do and vice versa. Ms. Lailanie Leachon, BNS, answered that women can bear children and can breast feed babies while men cannot.

 

 During the discussion of the gender division of labor, Ms. Rom-Leachon explained that the work of men and women can be categorized as productive, reproductive, and community activities. Productive activities are the paid activities done outside the home and in public arena while reproductive activities are the unpaid activities done inside the home. Meanwhile, community activities include the collective organization of social activities and services in the community.

 Moreover, the “Impossible Dream” video clip was also presented showing the issues faced by women like double-multiple workload, common situation of a family where both parents work outside the home, and economic marginalization with women being paid less than a husband who is devoting the same number of hours of work. In addition, all the household chores remain as woman's responsibility.

CvSU Gender and Development Resource Center (GADRC) sponsored a training-workshop on Gender Analysis and Referral and Case Management among MOVE officers and members on May 28-29, 2019 at Monte Vista Hotsprings and Conference Resort, Calamba City, Laguna.
